cse1_name: Legal Challenge to Google’s Cerrillos Data Center Environmental Approval
cse1_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse1_justif: This court ruling concerns a legal challenge to the environmental approval of Google’s Cerrillos Data Centre (~US$200M) initiated by a citizen group (MOSACAT) and the Municipality of Cerrillos (See https://web.archive.org/web/20260213210307/https://restofworld.org/2024/data-centers-environmental-issues/) and later continued by an individual claimant. The case questioned whether the environmental assessment adequately addressed water use air quality sustainability and the need for a full Environmental Impact Study. The ruling led Google to abandon the water-intensive design and relaunch the project with air-cooling significantly reducing its environmental impact.
cse2_name: TRAMAS - Geopolitics of the end of the world: rare earths in Penco Chile
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Environmental Impact
cse2_justif: The TRAMAS case study on Penco in central Chile conducted by the Non-profit Instituto Latinoamericano de Terraformación addresses AI’s environmental impact by examining extractive industries linked to digital and AI technologies. The “Mineral” section explains how rare earth elements are key inputs for semiconductors and digital infrastructure that enable AI while outlining environmental risks such as impacts on water ecosystems and local communities. These issues are further framed in sections on territory environmental conflict and community resistance in Penco which situate mineral extraction within global supply chains driven by data centres and AI-intensive industries.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Datos Protegidos collaboration in capacity building
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: On behalf of Datos Protegidos Foundation and the Transdisciplinary Digital Convergence Regulation Nucleus Patricia Peña collaborated in three multi-stakeholder events on AI gender equality and human rights. Her work exemplifies collaborative capacity building: presenting the feminist chatbot Sof+IA—developed by Datos Protegidos and the Observatory of Gender Statistics and Diversity (ODEGI)—at the 'Back to School with Artificial Intelligence' teacher training initiative while her roles at the IACHR hearing (representing the Feminist AI Research Network) and Khipu 2025 demonstrate sustained collaboration to advance gender justice in AI governance.
cse2_name: NGO Amaranta's Fanzine on AI
cse2_type: Other
cse2_contributions: Gender Equality
cse2_justif: The initiative involved the publication of the fanzine “La inteligencia artificial no es tan artificial ni tan inteligente” by NGO Amaranta a Chilean organisation focused on addressing online gender-based violence digital rights and feminist education. The fanzine strengthens AI literacy by offering accessible explanations of how AI systems work and emphasising that they are human-made technologies that can reproduce social and gender biases. It explicitly links AI and gender-based violence showing how AI systems and digital platforms can reinforce discrimination harassment sexism ableism and unequal power relations in online environments.
cse3_name: CENIA´s LatamGPT and other language models
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_justif: LatamGPT is a regional initiative led by the State funded CENIA in Chile that advances cultural and linguistic diversity in AI by developing a language model grounded in Latin America’s voices including Indigenous and local vernacular languages. Complementing this effort CENIA also leads the Rapa Nui Translator (https://www.traductorrapanui.cl/) created with the Academia de la Lengua Rapa Nui and UC which supports the preservation of the endangered Rapa Nui language through community-based AI tools. By countering the dominance of English-centric AI systems and fostering more inclusive and representative datasets and models LatamGPT contributes directly to the indicator on Cultural and Linguistic Diversity.

cse1_name: FIEL Foundation Workshop on AI and the World of Work
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  Labour Protections;  AI Literacy
cse1_justif: A workshop organised by Fundación Instituto de Estudios Laborales (FIEL) a non-profit created in 2005 by Chile’s main national trade union confederation the Central Unitaria de Trabajadores y Trabajadoras de Chile (CUT). It addressed labour protections through union-led adaptation to AI aiming to safeguard workers’ dignity and safety during digital transformation. It combines reskilling and upskilling efforts including analysis by CENIA on the impact of automation on occupations with a practical workshop on generative AI tools. AI literacy is further promoted through training union leaders on the challenges and opportunities of AI.

cse1_name: Advisory Committee against Disinformation
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Safety and Security;  Data Protection and Privacy
cse1_justif: The initiative developed with three civil society NGOs (Derechos Digitales Fundación Multitudes and Fundación Datos Protegidos) directly tackles AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence by recommending research and policies to counter threats like deepfakes particularly gendered disinformation (pp. 34-35). It addresses Data Protection and Privacy by urgently advocating for updated legislation and a supervisory agency to prevent the data exploitation that fuels disinformation campaigns (pp. 32). Furthermore it contributes to Safety and Security by proposing measures to protect critical infrastructure and coordinate institutional responses to disinformation threats during elections and public crises (example in p. 37).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: AI Safety and Governance Forum
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse1_justif: On July 27 2025 Concordia AI (a social enterprise based in Beijing) hosted a forum at the World AI Conference in Shanghai. The forum brought together a diverse group of experts to discuss AI safety and risk management. The forum includes the topic of 'transparency in technical disclosure and regulatory alignment [and] organization-level ethical governance mechanisms'. Discussions touched upon human oversight as well: they emphasized emergency shutdown systems hardware-enabled governance monitoring for deceptive behaviors and risk management frameworks (e.g. Frontier AI Risk Management Framework v1.0).
cse2_name: Frontier AI Risk Management Framework (v1.0)
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse2_justif: This is a report co-published by Concordia AI (social enterprise) and Shanghai AI lab (a state-affiliated AI lab) on AI risk. In the section on risk governance the report proposes 'Transparency and Social Oversight' as one way to govern AI risk. This includes model system card public oversight mechanisms third-party audits and supplementary liability mechanism (p.35) while Section 4.3 (p.26) addresses external communication of deployment decisions. The framework also introduces emergency control mechanisms (Section 6.4 p.35) red lines and deployment restrictions (Section 2) and lifecycle risk monitoring to ensure human oversight and prevent loss of control.
cse3_name: Responsible Innovation in AI x Life Sciences
cse3_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse3_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse3_justif: The report co-conducted with Concordia AI (social enterprise) promotes transparency through several recommendations:It suggests that researchers should be required to disclose the role of AI in their work and any potential biosecurity risks in research proposals and publications (6.4 p.80). It advocates for regular third-party audits of nucleic acid synthesis providers to enhance their risk controls (3.3.1 p.28).   The report stresses that human oversight is crucial especially in the AI-Enabled Offense-Defense Landscape (6.3.2. p.79)

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Cyber MulanAI Bean Plan
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: The AI Bean Plan jointly initiated by the Zhejiang Ant Foundation (a philanthropic group established by Ant Group) and the China Women's Development Foundation addresses gender equality through digital employment focusing on the AI data annotation industry. The project empowers women in economically underdeveloped regions through a 'training-certification-incubation-order provision' model. By the end of 2024 the 'Cyber Mulan  AI Bean Plan' had established 16 digital employment centers across various counties facilitating over 7500 local employment opportunities. Nearly 70% of participants were women achieving economic independence with average incomes significantly surpassing local per capita disposable incomes.
cse2_name: Disability-focused AI Knowledge Bases (First Batch)
cse2_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse2_contributions: Rights of Persons with Disabilities
cse2_justif: Tencent (big tech) partners with four Beijing CSOs (New Life Elderly and Disability Service Center Love Decibel Public Welfare Foundation Fengtai District Sound Wave Disability Social Service Center Xinmeng Autism Care Center) to create AI knowledge bases for people with visual hearing physical and autistic disabilities. These knowledge bases are built on an LLM and provide scientific authoritative knowledge on topics relevant to individuals with disabilities based on authoritative sources (including national accessibility standards to make information more accessible to a wider audience. The initiative also produced four AI assistants: 'No Barrier Pass 1.0' 'Golden Blind Cane' 'Little Shell Bunny' and 'Da Fu'.
cse3_name: Ethical Standards Consensus for Artificial Intelligence Services to Minors
cse3_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse3_justif: The China Cybersecurity Association (a social organization) in coordination with research institutions internet companies and legal organizations released ethical standards for AI services to minors. The consensus addresses children's rights in AI through seven principles: health priority (assessing AI's psychological impact on youth) content assurance (screening for violent/inappropriate content) privacy protection (highest data security standards for minors) clear rules (risk awareness education) transparency (accepting supervision) collaborative governance (multi-stakeholder cooperation) and ethics-first development. This industry self-regulation initiative guides AI providers on product design content management and privacy.

cse1_name: Voluntary AI Safety Commitments
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_justif: The AIIA (AI industry alliance a non-profit government-sponsored industry body) drafted Voluntary AI Safety Commitments that major Chinese model developers (e.g. Baidu Alibaba) signed. These commitments pledge safety measures red teaming and data security across the AI lifecycle (commitments 1& 2 p.3). This demonstrates an industry association (a form of CSO/mediator) collaborating to set and enforce non-binding governance standards that influence regulatory expectations.
cse2_name: AI Safety and Governance Forum at World AI Conference 2025
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: Safety and Security;  Data Protection and Privacy;  Data Sharing and Access;  Access to Redress and Remedy;  Impact Assessments;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse2_justif: The Concordia AI Safety and Governance Forum at WAIC 2025 is a high-level convening that addresses topics in AI safety. The initiative contributes to Data Protection and Privacy and Data Sharing and Access by featuring keynotes emphasizing the importance of trustworthy data sharing and privacy-preserving computation. It supports Access to Redress and Remedy by having experts recommend regulations for emergency shutdown mechanisms and defining 'red lines.' It contributes to Impact Assessments by discussing the need to mandate rigorous safety evaluations red-teaming and pre- and post-deployment monitoring. It tackled AI-facilitated misinformation with senior officials underscoring the urgency of governance over risks like 'deep fakes.'
cse3_name: Responsible Innovation in AI x Life Science
cse3_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse3_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Data Sharing and Access;  Safety and Security;  Access to Redress and Remedy;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Impact Assessments
cse3_justif: The Report is co-authored by CSO Concordia AI. Data Protection and Privacy: identifying data compliance as an optimization area for government agencies and suggesting synthetic data for privacy (2.1.1 pp.17-18); Data Sharing: advocating for a international warning and detection system for bio incidents that involve sharing data (6.3.1 p.79); Access to Redress and Remedy: noting that a lack of complete operation logs and attribution mechanisms hinders accountability and tracing responsibility (3.1.3 p.27); Impact Assessments: the report itself is a comprehensive risk classification and ethical evaluation summary; AI-facilitated misinformation: p. 38 on 'Information Viruses' (misinfo on biosci) that interfere with scientific consensus.

cse1_name: Media and Artificial Intelligence Literacy Research
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse1_justif: The research conducted by the Hong Kong Federation of Youth Groups (HKFYG) covers the media and AI literacy of youth. The survey found that 55.4% of the youth interviewed are concerned about personal data protection and privacy when using AI applications (p.1) where respondents shared concerns related to personal data leaks and the misuse of personal data  (p.1). Additionally the survey finds that youths face increasing online risks as AI-generated images and texts can propagate false information (p.3) but they may not have the skills to discern the authenticity of information (p.1). The HKFYG is sponsored by the Community Chest and receives government fundings but it is not part of the government.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: Fiscal Watson: Study on the Use of Artificial Intelligence in the Office of the Attorney General in Colombia - Karisma Foundation (2024)
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse1_justif: In its report on the Fiscal Watson tool of the Office of the Attorney General in Colombia the Karisma Foundation addresses equity and non-discrimination by warning that poor-quality data in the system for the oral accusatory criminal system can generate biased or erroneous results affecting the right to a fair trial (pp.14-15). It addresses transparency and explainability by criticising the opacity of Fiscal Watson the lack of public information about its operation and the contradictions in official responses (pp.18-20). Regarding human oversight and determination it emphasises that Watson should be a support tool whose results should be verified by prosecutors and not be definitive avoiding replacing human labour (p.16).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Digital environments for children and adolescents: what Bill 210 must not overlook - Karisma Foundation
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_justif: Karisma Foundation (2025 p.8) mentions in its comments and recommendations on the bill on safe digital environments that the “Comprehensive Monitoring and Evaluation System for the Protection of Children and Adolescents in Digital Environments” could include early detection technologies that use artificial intelligence or risk scoring systems. This could lead to mass surveillance preventive criminalization and discrimination especially toward children and adolescents who already face exclusion or stigmatization. The lack of algorithmic transparency informed consent and independent control mechanisms is highlighted which puts at risk rights such as privacy personal data protection and freedom of expression for children.
cse2_name: From the screen to public policy. Perspectives and challenges of cultural rights in digital environments in Colombia - Karisma Foundation (2025)
cse2_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse2_contributions: Gender Equality;  Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse2_justif: The report addresses among other things how artificial intelligence impacts cultural rights in digital environments. It analyzes the risks of algorithmic discrimination the invisibility of local cultural identities and the gender digital divide (Karisma Foundation 2025 pp. 24–25 and 45–46). It also proposes that Colombia participate in international debates on AI recommendation algorithms and platform regulation to ensure fair representation of diverse voices and protect cultural identity in the digital ecosystem (pp. 22–23 and 42–44).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: Statement by the Karisma Foundation before the First Committee of the House of Representatives - Karisma Foundation (2023)
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_justif: Karisma Foundation (2023) addresses Labour Protections by warning that Colombia’s role in AI supply chains often involves precarious work like data labeling and content moderation and urges regulation of automated decision systems that affect working conditions (pp.4-5 and 7). It supports AI Literacy indirectly by calling for a broad multi-stakeholder process to design context-aware regulation and segment risks fostering informed debate and understanding of AI impacts (pp.7-8).
cse2_name: VIII NATIONAL CONGRESS OF THE CUT - United Workers' Union of Colombia (2024)
cse2_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse2_contributions: Labour Protections
cse2_justif: During the 8th National Congress of the CUT (United Workers' Union of Colombia 2024) it was recognised that artificial intelligence and new technologies are profoundly transforming the world of work. The papers ‘Neoliberalism new technologies and the world of work’ (pp.25-27) ‘Decent work and just transition’ (pp.93-100) and ‘Working youth and trade union training’ (pp.147-152) recognised that these technologies can lead to job losses and labour flexibility with an increase in precariousness and loss of labour rights. They noted that automation and digital platforms displace workers affecting women and young people. They emphasised the need to ensure training and retraining so that workers can adapt to these changes.
cse3_name: How to live and survive as a journalist in Colombia - FLIP (2025)
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_contributions: Labour Protections;  AI Literacy;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives
cse3_justif: The FLIP (2025) report on the journalism sector addresses Labour Protections highlights precarious working conditions and recommends establishing ethical limits on the use of AI in order to protect jobs and editorial integrity (p.16). It addresses AI literacy and reveals that only 13% of journalists feel fully informed about AI calling for training in digital and AI skills (pp.10 and 16). Finally with regard to upskilling/reskilling it suggests promoting critical and strategic education on AI in order to adapt to digitalisation (p.16).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: Statement by the Karisma Foundation before the First Committee of the House of Representatives - Karisma Foundation (2023)
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Data Sharing and Access;  Safety and Security;  Consumer Protection;  Impact Assessments
cse1_justif: Karisma Foundation (2023) addressed trust and safety by highlighting gaps in data protection (p.6) urging harmonization with privacy laws and copyright exceptions for data access (p.5) and warning about security risks from foreign AI systems (pp.4–5). Them statement supported impact assessments and algorithmic audits but questioned institutional capacity (p.6).
cse2_name: Ruling T-323 of 2024 - Constitutional Court of Colombia (2024)
cse2_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Safety and Security
cse2_justif: Ruling T-323 of 2024 addresses the use of generative AI in judicial proceedings and establishes guidelines for its ethical and responsible use emphasizing transparency accountability privacy non-substitution of human rationality risk prevention equality human control and ethical regulation (para. 423). Civil society including Fundación Karisma and El Veinte provided a critical analysis of the risks of bias hallucinations and lack of data updates in AI and recommended that the judiciary develop its own AI to ensure data protection and reduce risks (paras. 255 and 258). These contributions influenced the Court's recommendations and orders to guarantee fundamental rights especially due process in the use of AI in the judiciary.
cse3_name: Fiscal Watson: Study on the Use of Artificial Intelligence in the Office of the Attorney General in Colombia - Karisma Foundation (2024)
cse3_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse3_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Data Sharing and Access;  Safety and Security;  Impact Assessments
cse3_justif: The report of Karisma Foundation (2024) analyzes Fiscal Watson’s use in the Office of the Attorney General in Colombia highlighting risks to privacy and data protection under Laws 1266 of 2008 and 1581 of 2012 (pp.5-7) issues of data sharing and sovereignty due to possible IBM cloud migration (pp.16–17) security measures like SPOA mirror copies (p.16). It also notes the absence of prior impact assessments and monitoring mechanisms (pp.23-24).

cse1_name: Women and Artificial Intelligence in the Congo
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: 'Women and Artificial Intelligence of Congo' (Fiac) is a network bringing together female entrepreneurs and students. It aims to train its members in artificial intelligence while establishing a mentoring system where each Fiac member mentors two to three students to pass on their expertise.The initiative addresses Gender Equality by increasing women’s participation and representation in artificial intelligence through targeted training mentorship networking and awareness-raising activities. FIAC empowers women and young girls to acquire AI skills access professional opportunities and engage in AI-related entrepreneurship and leadership thereby helping to reduce gender gaps in the digital and AI ecosystem in Congo-Brazzaville.

cse1_justif: The Junior Chamber International Congo (JCI-Congo) organized a high-level webinar during which it encouraged young people to embrace Artificial Intelligence (AI) to empower themselves. The areas it mainly contributes to are: Reskilling / upskilling initiatives – by providing guidance and exposure to AI tools and skills relevant to employment and entrepreneurship. AI Literacy – by raising awareness and understanding of AI concepts among youth participants.

cse1_name: Impact of generative AI on Costa Rican jobs
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_justif: The Academia Centroamericana a Costa Rican independent think tank published a study in March 2025 examining the impact of generative AI on jobs in Costa Rica. The study finds that 19.2% of Costa Rican jobs are susceptible to enhancement by generative AI technologies with a disproportionate impact on highly skilled positions (53%). It identifies the sectors most exposed and those least affected such as agriculture and transportation. The study is explicitly directed at informing policymakers and business leaders on how to mitigate risks and maximise benefits for the workforce making it directly relevant to the labour and skills implications of AI.

cse1_name: AI Under the Spotlight: The Other Side of the Coin
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: Privacy watchdog organization Politiscope organized an educational seminar for journalists and human rights defenders examining the societal impacts of artificial intelligence with particular emphasis on its implications for human rights and the right to non-discrimination. The event explored the often-overlooked harms of AI systems and how AI is shaping surveillance public services and policy making. It brought together several digital rights defenders who advocate and litigate to protect citizens from harms and discrimination inflicted by AI systems. The event concluded with a round table on the national implementation of the AI Act featuring digital rights activists and a representative of the Data Protection Authority.

cse1_name: Press release: 'Protecting children in online spaces is a shared responsibility of all those committed to the well-being of children'
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: On the occasion of the launch of the process for drafting the National Plan for the Development of Artificial Intelligence the civil society organization Pragma issued a press release calling for the introduction of new criminal offenses covering the creation possession and distribution of AI 'nudification' tools as well as the sharing or threatening to share explicit personal deepfake content. Pragma further demands that deepfake sexual abuse be formally recognized as a form of violence against children.

cse1_name: Media literacy workshop for journalism students
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: Democracy watchdog organization Gong holds workshops on disinformation for journalism students as part of its long-standing Media Literacy programme. A workshop held in May 2024 also addressed several AI-specific topics. The workshop examined the impact of artificial intelligence on the journalistic profession but also the impact AI-generated disinformation such as deepfake videos images and audio recordings. The ability to distinguish authentic content from AI-generated material was highlighted as an essential journalistic skill. As part of the programme participating journalists took part in a quiz designed to strengthen their ability to recognize AI-generated photographs.
cse2_name: Interplay of GDPR and the AI Act
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy
cse2_justif: The privacy watchdog organization Politiscope organized a panel discussion on 'The interplay of GDPR and the AI Act' as part of a regional Internet Governance Forum event (SEEDIG8). Representatives from the Office of the Ombudsperson Croatian Agency For Personal Data Protection and two Croatian unicorn companies Infobip and Rimac discussed key issues such as the legal basis for lawful personal data processing transparency requirements and data subject rights when personal data is processed by AI systems.
cse3_name: Promotion of regional cooperation and improving media standards
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_justif: The project “Encouraging Regional Cooperation and Improving Media Standards” implemented by the fact-checking organization Faktograf and funded by the National Endowment for Democracy (NED) aims to counter the misuse of new technologies that undermine democratic processes. Over a 15-month Faktograf monitors AI-generated content on online platforms. Faktograf produces investigative articles to help citizens better understand and recognise AI-generated disinformation.  The project also publishes in-depth analyses of the impact of artificial intelligence on society and democracy including its influence on elections labour and media integrity.

cse1_name: advocacy for Democratic and Inclusive Governance of Information in the Democratic Republic of Congo by Kinshasa News Lab
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_contributions: AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Safety and Security
cse1_justif: It explicitly links AI-related harms to trust and safety issues in the information ecosystem in the Democratic Republic of Congo (RDC). It highlights algorithmic manipulation disinformation deepfakes and emerging risks to public trust in information noting that such AI-enabled distortions undermine social cohesion and institutional confidence. The initiative advocates for a policy framework on AI use in media that incorporates transparency ethics and responsibility as well as coordinated mechanisms to counter disinformation and protect journalists in digital contexts — all central elements of trust and safety governance for AI systems interacting with public information channels

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: 5th Global Forum @ Casa de Campo: The Future of Artificial Intelligence: Innovation Ethics and Global Challenges.
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: This forum developed by FONGLODE (a local NGO) promotes ethical and sustainable AI development prioritizing public interest and multi-stakeholder collaboration. Key principles include accountability for roles and responsibilities and transparency. The forum advocates for AI that is inclusive accessible well-governed and empowers individuals. The keynote speech connects ethical risk management with sustainability goals. Through open debate clear explanations of risks and choices and balanced regulation. The event aims to guide AI development towards fair outcomes stable institutions and lasting benefits for society particularly in the face of geopolitical competition and rapid innovation.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: 5th Global Forum Casa de Campo: The Future of Artificial Intelligence: Innovation Ethics and Global Challenges.
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_contributions: Gender Equality;  Cultural and Linguistic Diversity;  Rights of Persons with Disabilities
cse1_justif: At the 5th Global Forum Casa de Campo: The Future of Artificial Intelligence: Innovation Ethics and Global Challenges organized by Global Democracy and Development Foundation (FUNGLODE) a Dominican NGO the opening speaker urged an AI future that is inclusive sustainable and accessible governed through multi-stakeholder collaboration so it empowers people rather than excludes them. They framed inclusion as core to ethical AI called for participation from government industry academia and civil society and flagged risks such as bias and job displacement that demand balanced regulation and ongoing public dialogue across health education culture and industry.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: Future of Hope
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: The “Future of Hope” program created and led by Cincinnatus and Altice boosts AI literacy among underserved youth through free training. The program covers core AI concepts critical thinking and practical coding culminating in certificates that validate participants’ new skills. By combining hands-on projects with problem-solving and collaboration it helps students understand apply and critically evaluate AI tools—essential literacies for both academic and professional paths. Delivered through a city–industry–NGO partnership the initiative expands equitable access to education beyond traditional schooling creates pathways to tech jobs and aligns with ENIA objectives such as curriculum-relevant skills and support for teachers.

cse1_name: First Edition of the Egypt's AI Bill Roundtable Discussion
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination;  Environmental Impact
cse1_justif: On January 14 2025 the Access to Knowledge for Development Center (A2K4D) hosted a high-level roundtable on Egypt’s draft AI Bill led by MP Sahar Al Bazar deputy chair of the Foreign Relations Committee. In collaboration with ADSERO Law Firm and APCO Worldwide the event brought together tech leaders multinational corporations entrepreneurs and academics to discuss balancing innovation with risk mitigation data governance and accountability in AI-driven decisions. The roundtable was the first in a series aiming to Charting Egypt’s Path to Responsible AI. The roundtable discussed the role of the bill in addressing the impact of AI on Fairness bias explainability human oversight and the environmental impact.
cse2_name: Free AI Training Program
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse2_justif: This program is brought by EiTESAL and Village Capital with support from Google and in partnership with MinnaLearn and the University of Helsinki. The training is composed of 4 chapters. Chapter 4 is about AI Implications. Examining the ethical social and business implications of AI implementation. Though which ethical components are tackled in the training is not clear since social implications are mentioned it contributes to fairness.
cse3_name: AI for People: An Event Co-organized with Kasr El-Dobara Evangelical Church (KDEC)
cse3_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse3_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Environmental Impact
cse3_justif: On April 26th Kasr El-Dobara Evangelical Church (KDEC) organized an event exploring the risks and opportunities of Artificial Intelligence (AI). The event was part of an ongoing initiative by the church called “Khatwa”—a service launched to deliver educational sessions aimed at helping individuals make more informed decisions in various areas of life. The event was divided into two main sessions: an educational session and a practical workshop. The educational session was led by Marwa Soudi Consultant and Gender Specialist for the MENA Observatory on Responsible AI. Marwa provided an in-depth overview of AI—its definition and its potential risks including environmental impact bias issues of transparency and fairness.
cse4_name: An Approach to AI Governance: Essential Frameworks for Promoting Justice and Protecting Human Rights by (Masaar Technology and Law community)
cse4_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse4_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Environmental Impact;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse4_justif: Masaar platform publishes policy papers and recommendations on AI governance standards and sectoral/regulatory approaches for Egypt.“Masaar” has introduced several papers and reports on AI. In particular the Ai governance policy paper  explicitly 'addresses the foundational principles necessary for ethical AI governance. It delves into key areas such as non-discrimination fairness and inclusivity emphasizing the importance of designing AI systems that do not perpetuate bias or exclude marginalized communities. It highlights the role of data integrity transparency and accountability in fostering trust and mitigating harm. Additionally it examines the critical need for human oversight....and environmental responsibilities'

cse1_name: The Organization for Women in Science for the Developing World (OWSD)- Egypt Chapter has organized a workshop entitled 'Women in Science and Artificial Intelligence for Shaping the Future'
cse1_drive: The evidence is not a document
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: OWSD (Organization for Women in Science for the Developing World) Egypt Chapter held a “Women in Science and Artificial Intelligence” workshop (Nov 19 2024) an explicit civil-society event linking gender and AI inclusion. The initiative promotes gender equality by empowering women scientists and researchers to actively participate and lead in the rapidly evolving field of AI. Through workshops expert talks and networking opportunities led by female role models. Supported by institutions like UNESCO and the National Research Center it also integrates gender perspectives into scientific research and policymaking ensuring women’s voices are represented in shaping the future of AI.

cse1_name: Elements of AI for Business
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy
cse1_justif: EiTESAL in partnership with Village Capital supported by Google and in collaboration with MinnaLearn and the University of Helsinki launched a free one-month online course titled “Elements of AI for Business” in Egypt. The curriculum spans four chapters AI fundamentals machine learning neural networks and AI implications designed for entry-level employees fresh graduates and young professionals.  Learners gain practical digital skills real-world business applications of AI and globally recognized training at no cost.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: Regulating Artificial Intelligence in Egypt: Proposed Standards and Principles by Masaar
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Impact Assessments;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Consumer Protection;  Safety and Security;  Access to Redress and Remedy
cse1_justif: The policy paper issued by Masaar an Egyptian NGO working at the intersection of technology and law does not introduce sector-specific legal provisions. Rather it assesses the broader political legal and regulatory environment in Egypt in relation to artificial intelligence with a particular focus on safety security data protection misinformation and fundamental rights. The report analyzes existing laws and regulatory frameworks and proposes a set of general evaluation criteria that should guide future AI legislation and define the obligations of AI system providers especially for high-risk applications. These criteria are intended to assess how AI systems may impact safety security privacy and consumer interests.

cse1_name: Best practices for the regulation of artificial intelligence systems in El Salvador
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse1_justif: The report 'Best Practices for the Regulation of Artificial Intelligence Systems in El Salvador' was prepared by the Salvadoran Foundation for Economic and Social Development in April 2025. It analyzes the legal and international frameworks for promoting AI in the region and presents recommendations for designing El Salvador's AI legal framework. On page 14 the document refers to the importance of incorporating principles as transparency and explainability Human Oversight and Determination. Fairness and Non Discrimination into the legal frameworks to be implemented in El Salvador drawing on best practices promoted by UNESCO.

cse1_justif: The seminar tackles “insufficient transparency” in AI use and trains ombudspersons to understand AI fundamentals (functions types underlying logic) and EU rules—so they can demand and assess explanations documentation and evidence in administrative decisions. Its stated goal is to improve readiness to provide effective oversight of AI used by public authorities—scrutinising automatic administrative decisions for rights compliance and ensuring human-led review and redress within ombuds mandates.

cse1_name: Development and roll-out of AI teaching materials and teacher training 2025 Safer Internet Day campaign in Estonia
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: The initiative is run by “Targalt internetis – Safer Internet Centre in Estonia” a multi-partner project coordinated by the Estonian Union for Child Welfare (NGO) together with the Education and Youth Board Tallinn University of Technology and the Estonian Social Insurance Board. Safer Internet Day 2025 (01.02–01.03.2025) was a nationwide awareness campaign on children’s digital literacy online safety and responsible use of AI and social media with school events webinars and materials on AI-related risks (misinformation design privacy).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: Political demands of Estonian trade union confederation for the 2024 European Parliament elections
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: The initiative is led by the Estonian Trade Union Confederation (EAKL) a national umbrella trade-union NGO representing workers. In the article EAKL hands over its political demands for the 2024 European Parliament elections to all parties and candidates. Among these are calls for a just digital transition regulation of AI based on a “human in control” principle and guaranteed access to lifelong learning for workers. Because EAKL uses these demands to shape parties’ programmes and future legislation this is coded as participation in public policy formulation.

It is a formal workshop for working teachers helping them understand and use AI tools in their daily work – this is AI literacy and upskilling of the education workforce.

It connects this to AI by promoting webinars and materials on algorithms manipulation scams artificial intelligence and misinformation and by offering themed videos on scams misinformation cyberbullying sexual abuse and AI.
