cse1_justif: This expert forum advises the Ministry of Innovation and escorts the government during the AI regulation process including Trust and Safety.
cse2_name: Mass Surveillance and the Applicability of the Right to Privacy in the Occupied Territories: A Legal Opinion
cse2_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Access to Redress and Remedy;  Safety and Security
cse2_justif: The legal opinion addresses Data Protection & Privacy Safety & Security and Access to Remedy by documenting invasive surveillance (“Surveillance Tools Used…”) analyzing the right to privacy under IHL and human rights law (“The Right to Privacy”; “1.1”; “2.1”) and showing how mass AI-driven monitoring violates dignity and ICCPR Art.17. It further explains the security harms created by biometric and digital surveillance (“The Violation of Human Rights”) and highlights the absence of oversight or avenues for redress within the dual legal system (“Israeli Law Applicable…”; “3.1”). This opinion was published online and included press coverage which increased public debate.
cse3_name: Preliminary Position Statement Regarding the Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the Work of Attorneys
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_contributions: Safety and Security;  Data Protection and Privacy;  Consumer Protection
cse3_justif: Data Protection and Privacy: The document mandates strict adherence to the Privacy Protection Law and attorney-client privilege. Attorneys are instructed to avoid inputting personal or confidential information into AI platforms to prevent data embedding and leakage.Safety and Security: It highlights security risks in both open and 'closed' systems noting that information can still leak. For internal tools firms must ensure vendors are contractually bound to confidentiality and data segregation.Consumer Protection: AI cannot replace professional judgment. Attorneys bear full liability for AI use and must verify all outputs to prevent misleading clients or courts. Explicit written consent from the client is required before using

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: LUMSA Foundation Summer School on Artificial intelligence ethics and human rights
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse1_justif: The LUMSA Human Academy Summer School is designed for university students postgraduates Ph.D. candidates professionals UN personnel and AI enthusiasts interested in exploring the intersection of AI ethics and human rights. It specifies that participants will engage with core values such as “non-discrimination transparency explainability meaningful human control”. It also highlights how issues like “algorithmic bias discrimination and inequalities in AI systems the tension between public safety and personal freedom” can be addressed.
cse2_name: The ethics of AI - Radio radicale symposium
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse2_justif: At Italy’s Senate Radio Radicale symposium speakers from government media professions and academia framed AI as a powerful tool that must be guided not halted. Priorities outlined were: defend copyright and the sustainability of journalism; make AI-made content clearly recognizable; combat deepfakes and doppelgänger sites; and accelerate digital skills. Professionals stressed ethics-by-design human oversight and disclosure to clients when AI is used. Scholars warned of biased or opaque models global failures and energy costs. The key transparency levers mentioned were: labeling watermarking data governance auditability and public education.
cse3_name: Privacy Network report 2024/2025 - 'Automated administration'
cse3_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse3_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse3_justif: The OAA launched by Privacy Network in 2021 maps and studies algorithmic decision-making in Italy’s public administration. The 2024 Privacy Network report 2024/2025 on 'Automated administration' promotes transparency by documenting which systems are in use and requiring disclosure of objectives datasets and outputs (pp. 18–19) addresses fairness by highlighting risks of bias and discriminatory outcomes e.g. in welfare education and immigration systems (pp. 9–10; 46–47). Futhermore it calls calls for accountability impact assessments and public involvement in design (pp. 12–15; 21) ensuring citizens can contest and understand automated decisions that involve them.
cse4_name: For a Responsible AI – White Paper by Fondazione Bassetti
cse4_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse4_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse4_justif: The For a Responsible AI white paper (2024) outlines ethical rules and concepts for AI governance emphasizing justice by highlighting the potential of bias in automated choices particularly against underrepresented groups (pp. 19–23). Furthermore encourages transparency by calling for explainable responsible AI and participatory government (pp. 24-27; 43-46) and stresses human oversight discussing the how AI must adhere to democratic ideals rights such as habeas corpus and accountability frameworks (pp. 30–33; 48).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Participation of the Digital Human Rights Network in Italian Senate hearings on national AI legislation
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: Rights of Persons with Disabilities;  Gender Equality
cse1_justif: The Digital Human Rights Network (Hermes Center Amnesty International Italia and other CSO) formally participated in Italian Senate hearings on the national AI law (DDL 1146) submitting a comprehensive memorandum addressing risks of algorithmic discrimination and exclusion including for persons with disabilities and marginalized groups. The memorandum recommends safeguards for digital rights and the establishment of an independent AI authority. (Memoria pages 1-14)

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: General Italian Labour Confederation (CGIL) Parliamentary hearing on the relationship between Artificial Intelligence and the world of work
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: Labour Protections;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives
cse1_justif: In this parliamentary hearing before the XI Labour Committee CGIL provided expert testimony on AI’s impact on labour calling for protections and structured training to accompany technological change. The memorandum stresses the need to “take action on work organization production processes professionalism and training” to manage AI-driven transitions and avoid inequality. CGIL advocated for social dialogue skills development and regulation to ensure a just transition for workers in the age of AI.
cse2_name: NIdiL CGIL Filcams CGIL and Filt CGIL Palermo (trade union) appeal against Glovo Group
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: Labour Protections
cse2_justif: The Foodinho (Glovo) “excellence score” created indirect discrimination: riders who could not work evenings/weekends/holidays (e.g. due to care responsibilities disability age or religious reasons) received lower scores and therefore lost access to better job opportunities including the ability to book preferred work slots in advance. The Palermo Labour Court held the system “blind” to individual circumstances and ordered a remediation plan after consultation with the unions (NIdiL CGIL FILCAMS CGIL FILT CGIL).
cse3_name: Coding Girls - Hackathon “Rethink. Artificial intelligence & design thinking at the service of the territory”
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_contributions: AI Literacy;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives
cse3_justif: The initiative by Mondo Digitale Foundation a knowledge-oriented non profit organization aims to spread awareness of the opportunities offered by generative artificial intelligence and provide skills for its sustainable and inclusive use with a particular focus on women and southern Italy. The programme involved a total of over 600 students 19 schools and 15 young tutors including master's students doctoral students and PhD students from the Universities of Salerno and Cagliari.
cse4_name: Stati Generali dell’Innovazione – AI Conference on Work Regulation & Skills (Dec 2024)
cse4_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse4_contributions: AI Literacy;  Labour Protections
cse4_justif: This national conference organized by Stati Generali dell’Innovazione on 7 December 2024 explored the implications of AI for society labour and future skills coinciding with adoption of the EU AI Act. Speakers analyzed regulation workplace impacts and the “new digital divide in the age of AI” addressing inequalities between workers and the need for awareness and adaptation in employment and education contexts.
cse5_name: 'Prospects on Risks Liabilities and Artificial Intelligence Empowering Robots at Workplace Level' - Brodolini Foundation Working paper
cse5_justif: This initiative directly advances the Labour and Skills area by examining risks and protections for workers interacting with AI-empowered robots. The paper by the Brodolini Foundation frames AI/robot systems as a “third element” in employment relations and analyzes workplace risks liability and safety duties under EU and U.S. regimes (Introduction; Sec. 1–4). It focuses on injury prevention insurance and mitigation (Sec. 3) questions adequacy of existing occupational safety systems and proposes a new “Robot Labor Law” to regulate IWRs and protect workers’ wellbeing (Sec. 5) - Link https://www.fondazionebrodolini.it/en/story

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: AI Governance and Consumer Protection - banking financial and insurance authorities - Consumers' Forum
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_contributions: Consumer Protection;  Safety and Security;  Data Protection and Privacy
cse1_justif: The 'Governance dell'AI e tutela degli consumatori' initiative of the Consumers' Forum. Italy's independent authorities (AGCM AGCOM Garante Privacy IVASS and Bank of Italy) convened under the title 'Il ruolo delle Autorità bancarie finanziarie e assicurative' (Rome April 2025) to evaluate the risks and protections associated with the use of AI in banking insurance and financial services. By emphasizing consumer rights openness and monitoring in algorithmic decision-making it improves consumer protection tackles privacy issues and encourages the safer application of AI in delicate economic areas.
cse2_name: When They See Us – Exhibition on AI and Biometric Surveillance
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Safety and Security;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse2_justif: The exhibition When They See Us (Bologna Sept. 2024) examined the ways in which biometric systems powered by AI track categorize and possibly discriminate against people. Interactive pieces such as Tactical Tech's visualizations revealing the collection and use of data and Dries Depoorter's facial recognition simulation installations were on display. It promoted critical public discourse on surveillance digital rights and the dangers of AI misuse in misinformation and security contexts. It was organized under Sineglossa's 'The Next Real' program (Sept. 2024–June 2025) and co-curated with The Good Lobby and Hermes Center.
cse3_name: IDMO (Italian Digital Media Observatory) – Monthly Report on AI-Generated Disinformation (April 2025 edition)
cse3_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse3_justif: The research published in April 2025 which was produced by Pagella Politica/Facta in collaboration with Bufale.net Open and other fact-checkers tracks how AI techniques increase the likelihood of false information. AI-generated misinformation in Italy quadrupled from the previous month to 11.6% of misleading reports according to the IDMO monthly report. Fake photos and videos of Pope Francis as well as misleading statements on the EU Ukraine and vaccines were among the subjects. The aim is to increase public awareness encourage fact-checking and fortify resistance against AI-driven manipulation by monitoring narratives and uncovering fake information.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: WE-TALK
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The WE-Talk project a collaboration between WMW Jamaica Violence Prevention Alliance and Oxfam Canada used AI to analyze 245000 Google queries and 16000+ social media posts to gain insights into perceptions and attitudes on online gender-based violence (OGBV) in Jamaica. WMW Jamaica worked directly with Quilt AI to customise algorithms for Jamaica's cultural context including Jamaican Patois. Using these insights WE-Talk crafted targeted campaigns and workshops to tackle GBV root causes including the ongoing WE-Talk FACTSS awareness campaign (February 2024).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: JCTU demands people-first policies as AI reshapes labour market
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_contributions: Labour Protections;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy
cse1_justif: The Jamaica Confederation of Trade Unions (JCTU) has issued public statements calling for 'people-first' AI policies demanding worker consultation training and protections as AI and automation reshape the labour market. JCTU Vice President Shamir Brown speaking at the Planning Institute of Jamaica's labour market forum 'AI and Us: Navigating the New Human-Machine Partnership' (September 24 2025) stated that AI deployment must be guided by fairness equity and dignity and that workers must have a voice in how AI is introduced into their workspaces. The JCTU is calling for increased training programmes to help employees transition and remain competitive in the evolving workplace.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: Initiatives for AI Ethics by JEITA Members Aiming to Promote Ethical Development and Use of AI
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse1_justif: The Japan Electronics and Information Technology Industries Association (JEITA) a non-gov. industry association formed by merger of 2 associations (JEIDA and EIAJ) focuses on the realization of Society 5.0 and providing policy recommendations to the JP Gov. JEITA prepared a material on a network of initiatives for AI ethics being carried out by various JEITA members in a joint effort collaboration. Major private sector actors outlined in their respective document best practices of AI [pp.5-6] to reduce unwanted side-effects such as biases that might affect judgments of AI which could result in discrimination [p.2]. Many of the initiatives also emphasized the need for transparency [pp.4-5 9] in light of digital safety and accountability.
cse2_name: Press Conference by the Citizens' Association
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Environmental Impact
cse2_justif: The Citizens’ Association publicly raised concerns about Mitsui Fudosan’s plan to construct a 72-meter data center in Hino City. Mitsui Fudosan is a major Japanese real estate developer known for large-scale commercial/urban projects. The association held a press conference urging both the city to establish a formal consultation forum and require developers to adopt measures preventing harm to the environment and daily life. Citing unresolved concerns over groundwater local water resources shade electromagnetic waves electricity use and CO2 emissions the Citizens’ Association demanded detailed environmental disclosure to enforce environmental goals (such as reducing greenhouse gases) and ensure constructive transparent discussions.
cse3_name: Column: Growth of AI Will Not Negatively Impact Electricity Supply and Demand Even Help to Achieve 100% Renewable Energy
cse3_type: Other
cse3_justif: The Renewable Energy Institute is a public interest incorporated foundation that conducts studies on electricity systems and renewable-energy policy. Their research column on AI and energy indicates that a potential rise in demand from increased AI use can be mitigated through wider adoption of renewables improved energy-management strategies and advances in semiconductor efficiency; measures that together reduce greenhouse-gas emissions and shrink AI’s environmental footprint. They also note that if a higher-demand era arrives due to AI sustainable progress of Japan will depend on supplying electricity at near-zero marginal generation cost so AI’s expansion might actually help accelerate Japan’s transition toward 100% renewable power.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: B’AI Global Forum: Research on norms ethics and praxis for a truly gender equal society and a guarantee of rights for minorities in the AI age? (Beyond AI)
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_contributions: Rights of Persons with Disabilities;  Cultural and Linguistic Diversity;  Gender Equality
cse1_justif: The B’AI Global Forum project is structured around four pillars. Pillar 1 focuses on supporting gender equality by working toward a gender-equal society researching AI-mediated discrimination and examining equality attitudes toward disabled people. Pillars 2 and 3 aim to design culturally diverse and inclusive media-expression spaces with AI and to create a global forum for collaboration. They explicitly emphasizes under point 3 the creation of a global forum for collaboration between the humanities and sciences bringing together industry government academia and the private sector and fostering a broad network of research and practice across national boundaries by for example holding workshops.
cse2_name: International Women’s Day Special Event: Inclusion Through Innovation: Can AI Bridge the Gender Gap in Society? Hosted by The College Women’s Association of Japan (CWAJ)
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: Gender Equality;  Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse2_justif: The College Women’s Association of Japan (CWAJ) invited leading researchers to discuss AI’s potential to hinder and to promote gender equality in Japanese society. The first talk on gendered innovations presented the failure of scientific research historically to serve women and minority groups. The second highlighted the potential of AI to identify and correct gender biases in hiring processes. The third discussed the broader implications of AI on social inequality noting how data biases could worsen disparities and proposing ways to make AI more inclusive and equitable for all. While the main focus was on Gender Equality the first and third talk were more broad and also touched on cultural diversity (minority groups social inequality).
cse3_name: National Awareness Survey on ''Generative AI and Human Rights Violations of Children'' (Child Fund Japan)
cse3_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse3_justif: Child Fund Japan surveyed public attitudes on how generative AI/deepfakes can potentially violate children’s rights (bullying/harassment child sexual abuse material (CSAM) privacy) and used the findings for advocacy of stronger legal protections (e.g. banning synthetic/“non-real” child sexual abuse material as well as real-child material see p. 6) plus education/self-protection measures for youth.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: Recommendations for the Information and Technology Working Group of the Curriculum Planning Special Subcommittee of the Central Council for Education – Next Curriculum Guidelines
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The specified NPO (see: https://code.or.jp/news/2025) Minna no Code (Translation: Everyone’s Code) which promotes digital education for children released “Recommendations for the Curriculum Planning Special Subcommittee of the Central Council for Education - Next Curriculum Guidelines.” Drawing on research and school collaborations the proposal outlines how information education can foster creativity and technological literacy while ensuring compatibility with emerging technologies such as GenAI. It emphasizes positioning AI literacy in the curriculum [pp. 4-5] and calls for timely revisions of curriculum guidelines to respond flexibly to rapid technological advances contributing directly to Japan’s ongoing education policy discussions.
cse2_name: JMITU Statement on settlement of the case for unfair labor acts(misuse of artificial intelligence)
cse2_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse2_contributions: Labour Protections
cse2_justif: In August 2024 the Japan Metal Manufacturing Information and Telecommunications Workers’ Union (JMITU) and IBM Japan reached a landmark settlement at the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Labor Relations Commission concerning IBM’s use of its in-house AI system IBM Watson in salary assessments. JMITU had challenged IBM’s refusal to disclose how AI influenced wage decisions citing risks of bias and opacity. The agreement obliges IBM to reveal all AI-related factors explain their link to wage criteria and negotiate transparently on future concerns. This precedent enhances employee protection promoting transparency and accountability in the corporate use of AI for HR and pay evaluation.
cse3_name: Official launch of the 'Everyone Generates AI Course' that can be used in school classes (BETA)
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_justif: Minna no Code who are a certified non-profit organization focused on digital education launched the Beta of the 'Everyone Generates AI' Course. The course targets elementary junior high and high schools across the country aiming at creating an environment where generated AI can be used safely and securely in schools and classes with practical teaching support (on-demand videos lesson examples teacher management). The course places the utmost importance on expanding students' free ideas through generative AI assuring that by allowing students to experience generated AI themselves under the appropriate guidance of a teacher they will be able to understand its characteristics including its advantages and disadvantages.
cse4_name: Code for Japan Summit 2024
cse4_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse4_contributions: AI Literacy
cse4_justif: The Code for Japan Summit is an event where speakers and participants can be inspired by keynotes learn about activity examples think through workshops and interact with each other. The event is organized by Code for Japan who are a non-profit group that brings together technologists civic activists and community members to build open-source tools support civic participation and promote digital inclusion across Japan. One of the sessions during the summit was specifically focused on the grassroots introduction of cutting-edge technologies such as metaverse generation AI in educational institutions.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: AI Alignment Network - Webinar Series
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The AI Alignment Network's primary activity is to cultivate and support AI alignment research. In the face of a researcher shortage both domestically and abroad they strengthen the overall field by driving forward both education and research in the field. To this end they created a Webinar Series regularly inviting expert speakers. Many of the Webinars are focused on topics related to AI safety such as discussing Safeguards for AI (Webinar #11) and the field of general AI with safety guarantees (Webinar #3).
cse2_name: Progress in AI data utilization and required governance - Conference (MyData Japan)
cse2_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Safety and Security;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Consumer Protection
cse2_justif: The conference hosted by the Japan Information Economy and Society Promotion Association tackled several topics regarding AI data utilization. Specifically the experts talked about safety management measures and security risks of AI (Section 1-2) the damage caused by AI hallucinations and false information output (Section 1-2)  privacy violations (Section 1-2) the importance of businesses responding to privacy issues and being accountable to consumers (Section 1-1) and AI governance and its relationship with privacy governance (Section 1-2).
cse3_name: Fact Check - White Paper
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_justif: The Fact Check - White Paper  jointly created by the certified NPO Fact Check Initiative (FIJ) and Waseda University Next Generation Journalism and Media Research Institute (INGJM) addresses many of the false or misinformation incidents comprehensively describe the history current situation and challenges of fact-checking efforts in Japan. Among the discussed facts was also the case of generative AI being used to construct fake images of heavy rain disasters to gain attention on social media [p. 21]. The paper concluded that  due to the advent of generative AI it is expected that false and misinformation will be easily produced in large quantities in the future and further strengthening of fact-checking will be required [p. 100].
cse4_name: US-Japan NEXT Alliance Initiative - Allied on AI Assurance (Roundtable Dialogue)
cse4_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse4_contributions: Safety and Security;  Impact Assessments
cse4_justif: The US-Japan NEXT Alliance Initiative established by Sasakawa Peace Foundation USA with support from The Nippon Foundation is a forum for bilateral dialogue and joint policy recommendations. They held a roundtable dialogue to discuss matters of allied AI Assurance proposing best practices to move forward. They discussed and compared different approaches to AI safety [p. 6] and strategies for AI security [p. 8] but also the process of risk assessment arguing that we must anticipate future developments in the AI field and build a comprehensive understanding of its risks [p. 9] and also recommended AI assurance practices incident sharing initiatives and risk and vulnerability analysis sharing [p. 10].

cse1_name: Digital Shadows: The Threat of Online Violence Against Women in Jordan
cse1_type: Other
cse1_justif: The Arab Renaissance for Democracy and Development (ARDD) published a 2024 blog and assessment project on Technology-Facilitated Gender-Based Violence (TFGBV) in Jordan analyzing how generative AI tools like deep-fakes intensify online harassment of women. Drawing on national data and testimonies ARDD links AI misuse to gender inequality urges stronger legal safeguards and localizes global TFGBV debates. The initiative advances gender equality promotes digital inclusion and raises awareness of AI-enabled gendered harms in Jordan’s digital sphere.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: Phenix Center concludes training on empowering youth to work on cloud platforms.
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy
cse1_justif: In 2025 the Phenix Center for Economic Studies and Informatics a Jordanian non-profit CSO conducted a training program for 60 youth from Amman Zarqa and Balqa on essential AI and digital-work skills. Participants learned to use AI tools such as ChatGPT Copilot and Gemini and to apply them in freelancing content creation and cloud platforms like Mostaql. The program promoted AI literacy employability and digital inclusion supporting youth reskilling for the evolving labour market through practical ethical use of AI technologies.
cse2_name: Harnessing Technology and Artificial Intelligence: An Opportunity for Jordan to Boost Productivity
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy;  Labour Protections
cse2_justif: The Jordan Strategy Forum (JSF) a non-profit think tank published “Harnessing Technology and Artificial Intelligence: An Opportunity for Jordan to Boost Productivity” (March 2024) to enhance public and institutional awareness of AI’s role in national productivity. In pages 16 to 18 “Forum Recommendations” the JSF urges joint public–private–civil society efforts to launch a national awareness campaign and training workshops on AI concepts and ethical use targeting all age groups companies and public institutions. It further calls to integrate AI tools and ICT/STEM skills into education at all levels and to provide grants and specialized training to develop a skilled labor force as well as covering labor protection in Section 2 page 7

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: Advancing AI Governance and Security in Jordan
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The Information Systems Audit and Control Association (ISACA) Amman Chapter hosted the seminar “Advancing AI Governance and Security in Jordan” on 13 February 2025 engaging 40–50 professionals in discussions on AI risk management cybersecurity and ISO 42001 (International Organization for Standardization) the international standard for AI management and governance. Through expert-led sessions by Société Générale de Surveillance (SGS) participants learned how to identify assess and mitigate AI-related risks align with ethical and security standards and strengthen compliance practices. The initiative promoted a culture of AI safety accountability and trust across Jordan’s digital ecosystem.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: #SheInnovator – AI Training Program for Women and Girls (TechnoWomen)
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: CSO TechnoWomen initiated and implemented the #SheInnovator program training over 1650 women across 10 regions in AI and digital transformation. The initiative directly advances Fairness and Non-Discrimination by increasing women’s participation in AI promoting gender-inclusive data practices and reducing algorithmic bias. TechnoWomen also joined the KazLLM Competence Center to develop a gender component in AI including bias detection discrimination audits and inclusive model design. '... representation of women in AI are critical to the development of inclusive and unbiased Technologies. Diversity in AI Teams leads to a wider range of perspectives and experiences which can help avoid bias in data algorithms and models (p. 3).'
cse2_name: The Digital Almaty 2025 Forum: Women in Tech Kazakhstan’s panel “Gender-Biased AI: Technologies for a New Era”
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse2_justif: The panes was organized by CSO Women in Tech Kazakhstan Chapter. This public session as part of Central Asia’s largest tech conference drew attention to systemic gender biases in AI and explored pathways toward inclusive innovation. It featured Kazakhstani and international experts highlighting issues like biased language models and the lack of women in AI while advocating solutions (from balanced datasets to institutionalizing inclusivity in upcoming AI laws). Such high-profile events in 2025 raised public awareness and spurred dialogue on ethical AI signaling strong civil society engagement.

cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The forum is a CSO-initiated multi-stakeholder event organized by the Kazakhstani NGO TechnoWomen with support from the National Commission on Women’s Affairs ITU the Ministry of Digital Development Astana IT University and Astana Hub. TechnoWomen as the lead CSO designed and delivered the programme including AI masterclasses attended by around 300 women and showcased its IT-Ayel and TechGirls initiatives. The forum also highlighted CSO contributions through the TechnoWomen Leaders awards recognising groups such as the Kinergy Public Foundation and the National Association of Women Entrepreneurs ASMAR.

cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: Women in Tech Kazakhstan a non-profit promoting women’s participation in STEM and AI organized the “AI for Her – Meet-up Pro” on 20 June 2025 as a follow-up event to the online course “AI Tools for Personal Effectiveness” launched earlier that month. The meet-up provided practical AI training through sessions on effective AI tools and creating landing pages using AI. It strengthens women’s employability by building hands-on AI skills digital competencies and professional networks.
cse2_name: Standard for the protection of workers’ rights in the Implementation of Artificial Intelligence in enterprises
cse2_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse2_contributions: Labour Protections
cse2_justif: The Federation of Trade Unions of the Republic of Kazakhstan together with the Council of Trade Unions of Central Asian countries developed a Standard for the protection of workers’ rights in the implementation of AI in enterprises. The document sets out human-centred and socially just principles for workplace AI including prohibitions on algorithm-only dismissals (Section 4.1) requirements for transparency and explainability of AI decisions (Section 3.1–3.4) and mandatory social dialogue with worker representatives (Sections 1.6 2.1–2.4). It is intended as a practical model for legislation collective agreements and sectoral standards promoting retraining and decent work in AI-driven digitalisation (Sections 5.1–5.5 9.1).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: FactCheck Dive Offline Training Series – MediaNet International Center for Journalism (2024–2025)
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: The project is implemented by MediaNet International Center for Journalism and including trainers from Factcheck.kz. The initiative strengthens resilience against AI-facilitated misinformation by training journalists students and educators to detect misinformation verify digital content and identify AI-generated text images and videos. Participants learned OSINT methods advanced search techniques and digital verification tools. The description explicitly notes sessions on “identifying AI-generated content” and fact-checking practices. The program builds national media literacy and resilience against AI-driven misinformation. The event date is given at https://web.archive.org/web/20250806083404/https://medianet.ngo/en/archives/5942
cse2_name: XV Central Asian Forum “Development of the Internet Sphere in Central Asia – InternetCA-2024”
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse2_justif: This event was organized by CSO - the International Center for Journalism MediaNet. The initiative contributes to Public Engagement & Awareness by convening journalists civil society researchers government officials and students to discuss digital rights and AI-related challenges. The forum addressed strategies to counter disinformation the impact of neural network services (AI) and approaches to regulating the internet while safeguarding rights. Sessions focused on balancing regulation with rights combating propaganda and understanding AI-driven trends in the internet sphere.
cse3_name: AIFC–Digital Rights Center Qazaqstan guidance on data protection & AI
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_justif: In 2024 Digital Rights Center Qazaqstan in partnership with the Office of the Commissioner for Data Protection at the Astana International Financial Centre (AIFC) co-developed and published a publicly accessible Guidance on Data Protection and the Use of Artificial Intelligence. The document gives practical recommendations to organisations on how to process personal data in AI systems including transparency security and accountability obligations and is explicitly intended as a tool to mitigate AI-related privacy and security risks in the financial sector and beyond. As co-author DRCQ contributed legal and civil-society expertise on privacy and digital rights.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: Policy Brief - AI for Inclusive Development: Guiding Kenya's Policy Landscape
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse1_justif: This policy brief examines the challenges and solutions guiding Kenya’s policy landscape towards inclusive development and ethical AI integration for societal benefits.It provides key recommendations for forging Kenya's path towards responsible AI leadership. It recommends development of ethical AI principles for building trust in AI systems. These include among others fairness and transparency.
cse2_name: Media Guide on the Use of Artificial Intelligence in Kenya
cse2_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse2_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse2_justif: The Guide establishes that media houses and journalists must deploy AI tools while preserving human judgment and responsibility. Key ethical principles include transparency (identifying automated content) accountability (who is responsible when AI produces errors or bias) privacy and data protection (respecting personal data complying with laws like the Data Protection Act) fairness (avoiding discrimination or bias) and intellectual property rights. For sustainability it emphasizes investing in skills and capacity (talent-development) continuous performance measurement risk management and strategic implementation so media organisations can adapt and remain viable long-term.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: AMNESTY INTERNATIONAL KENYA AND 5RIGHTS FOUNDATION COMMENTS THE DRAFT GUIDANCE NOTE FOR PROCESSING CHILDREN’S DATA
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_justif: This is a Memorandum developed by Amnesty International Kenya during the public participation phase of the Draft Guidance for Processing Children's Data prepared by Kenya's Data Protection Authority (DPA). In this Memorandum Amnesty recommend that the DPA enhances the Guidance Note by clearly defining and regulating the commercial use of children’s data; strengthening provisions for children’s evolving capacities and direct participation in data rights; emphasising tech companies’ responsibility to protect children’s rights rather than relying on parental consent mechanisms; addressing the additional risks presented by AI systems; and explicitly integrating a broader human rights impact assessment framework that considers online harms.
cse2_name: Report: Generative AI and the Rights of Children in Kenya.
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Children’s Rights
cse2_justif: This policy brief based on in-depth conversations with 15 children (ages 13–17) across Kenya reveals how GenAI is shaping young lives in ways that are both inspiring and alarming. It highlights the risks children experience daily which include;algorithmic bias cognitivie over-reliance privacy fears misinformation dangers and inequality. These Policy Brief provides actionable recommendations on child-centric policies for policy makers and tech companies.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: Press Release by the Kenya Human Rights Commission: Why government must do more to protect dignity and human rights of digital platform workers
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: In its March 2024 press release “Taptengelei: Why Government Must Do More to Protect Dignity and Human Rights of Digital Platform Workers” the Kenya Human Rights Commission (KHRC) condemned poor working conditions in Kenya’s gig economy highlighting exploitation low pay and lack of social protection for platform workers. KHRC urged the government to regulate the sector ensure fair pay provide legal safeguards and uphold workers’ dignity and human rights within Kenya’s rapidly growing digital and platform-based labour markets. It addressed cases like Sama's subcontracting for AI labelling in Kenya

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: Judicial Review Application E119 of 2023
cse1_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse1_justif: In 2023 5 Kenyan CSOs - Katiba Institute Law Society of Kenya Kenya Human Rights Commission International Commission of Jurists-Kenya (ICJ Kenya) and the African Center for Open Governance were ex-parte applicants in a litigation that challenged World Coin Foundation's collection of Kenyans' data including biometric data through facial scans without prior consent on the basis that it breached provisions in the data privacy Act of 2019. Key breaches include consent transfer of personal data outside of Kenya collection of sensitive data data security. The case is particularly relevant to AI as it challenges data privacy issues in the context of biometric data and facial recognition system. The court ruled on the case in May 2025.

cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.

cse1_justif: The Workshop was organized by the Journalists’ Public Association (a Kyrgyz CSO promoting media freedom and digital literacy see:  https://web.archive.org/save/https://journalist.kg/ru/about/) with support of UNESCO. It featured expert presentations on legal and ethical regulation of AI human rights protection and algorithmic accountability. A practical session demonstrated bias and variability in AI outputs underscoring the need for explainability and responsible human oversight.

cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.

cse2_justif: The research by ISOC Kyrgyz Chapter and KG Labs Public Foundation promotes awareness on Fairness and Non-Discrimination by advocating open-source and publicly licensed AI models that ensure equal access and reduce algorithmic bias in government digital systems. It promotes awareness on Transparency and Explainability by requiring publication of AI data code and model documentation allowing external review and public understanding of AI decision-making. Human Oversight and Determination by recommending governance mechanisms and expert working groups to guide review and ethically monitor AI integration in public digital infrastructure. This initiative inform the public about responsible AI principles.

It organizes events conferences and trainings including mentioned initiative - participation in the Turkic States AI Summit 2024 where co-founder Timur Turatali presented on “Artificial Intelligence and National Languages: Preserving and Developing the Linguistic Heritage of the Turkic World.” The initiative explicitly connects AI with the promotion of linguistic and cultural diversity including supporting the development of Kyrgyz-language AI tools such as AkylAI.

The initiative culminated in the final competition on 10 Nov 2023 where winning teams received sponsorship to develop their AI and digital projects. cse1_justif: The Responsible AI Governance Programme by the Internet Society Kyrgyz Chapter (a Kyrgyz CSO) began in 2023 as indicated by its AI-related activities on its website (https://web.archive.org/save/https://isoc.kg/news/page/5/) and remains ongoing in 2025. It promotes ethical and transparent AI development through a rights-based approach. It addresses Safety and Security through regular monitoring auditing and oversight to ensure AI reliability and prevent harm. Impact Assessments by promoting continuous evaluation of AI systems’ societal and ethical implications throughout their lifecycle. The initiative delivers capacity building through training programs on AI deployment and AI-related laws for civil servants judiciary membersCSOsetc.
cse2_name: Workshop on Legal Aspects of the Use of Artificial Intelligence Held in Bishkek
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Safety and Security
cse2_justif: The workshop featured expert presentations on legal and ethical regulation of AI human rights protection and algorithmic accountability. On data protection and privacy: During the workshop participants explicitly discussed the risks of unauthorised access to and leakage of official or confidential information when AI systems are used by government officials highlighting the need for legal safeguards and appropriate regulation. On Safety and security: speakers also addressed the use of AI for fraud and other illegal activities as well as associated national security risks and emphasised the need for state oversight and balanced regulation to protect the population.

cse1_justif: Maharat Foundation’s AI and Digital Rights program promotes Fairness and Non-Discrimination by training journalists and civil society actors to identify and counter bias in automated decision-making and online moderation systems. It also advances Transparency and Explainability by emphasizing algorithmic accountability media responsibility and open communication about how AI systems influence access to information. Through ongoing workshops and collaborations with SMEX and UNESCO the initiative builds capacity for ethical and transparent AI use in Lebanon.

cse2_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse2_justif: Bread&Net 2024 organized by SMEX served as a major regional platform for digital rights and AI ethics discussions. It included panels on algorithmic accountability bias in automated systems and AI-driven misinformation. The initiative promoted fairness transparency and accountability in AI and digital governance engaging activists policymakers and researchers from across Lebanon and the MENA region through open public dialogue and awareness events.
cse3_name: SMEX – From Sharing to Silence: Assessing Social Media Suppression in the WANA Region (2024)
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse3_justif: This SMEX research report examines how automated content moderation and platform algorithms disproportionately suppress voices from the WANA region including Lebanon. It analyses algorithmic bias lack of transparency in moderation systems and unequal redress mechanisms. By documenting these systemic issues the initiative promotes fairness and accountability in AI-driven moderation and advocates for transparent human-rights-based digital governance.

cse1_name: 'Justice for IPN comrades.' Call for collective action after ruling in favor of Diego N..
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_justif: In December 2024 the Frente Nacional para la Sororidad Defensoras Digitales del Movimiento Ley Olimpia and the Asamblea Justicia Digital IPN organised collective actions in response to a judge's acquittal of a student found with 166000 AI-generated images and 20000 videos produced for the sexual exploitation of female IPN students. The organisations publicly demanded judicial accountability ethical limits on AI-generated content and full application of the Ley Olimpia — raising public debate on human oversight and the ethical use of AI in criminal contexts.
cse2_name: UNESCO-ANIA AI Readiness Assessment for Mexico (RAM Report)
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse2_justif: In July 2024 ANIA co-developed with UNESCO the 'Readiness Assessment Methodology (RAM) Report for Mexico' presented at the Senate of the Republic. The report produced through 18 multi-stakeholder roundtables with over 250 participants provides a comprehensive diagnosis of Mexico's preparedness for ethical AI governance across legal sociocultural scientific and economic dimensions. It addresses AI ethics and sustainability directly: it identifies governance gaps recommends an AI ethics-centred national strategy and proposes institutional design for responsible AI development aligned with UNESCO's Recommendation on the Ethics of AI.

cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: In July 2025 Mexican voice actors and dubbing artists organised public demonstrations in Mexico City under the slogan 'Real Voices for a Humane Future' demanding legislative protection against AI-driven voice cloning and job displacement. Demonstrators called for recognition of the voice as sensitive biometric data fair compensation when voices are used to train AI systems protection of deceased artists' rights and the creation of a 'Dubbing Made in Mexico' identity. The initiative directly addresses labour protections for workers in sectors affected by generative AI.

cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  Labour Protections;  AI Literacy
cse2_justif: In May 2024 ANIA published the 'Propuesta de Agenda Nacional de IA para México 2024–2030' in collaboration with UNESCO and over 40 civil society organisations. Section 3.2 addresses the Labour and Skills dimension directly: Objective 3.2.1 covers education digital skills development and workforce preparation for AI-driven labour market transformation; Objective 3.2.2 explicitly addresses AI literacy through educational goals and public awareness; and the document incorporates ethical considerations and protections for workers in AI employment contexts. The agenda provides concrete policy recommendations across all three Labour and Skills indicators: labour protections reskilling and upskilling and AI literacy.

cse1_name: Artificial Intelligence in Warfare: Evolution of Protection or Threat to Ethics?
cse1_drive: This evidence does not consist of a document.
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse1_justif: The article was published by the CSO Women4Cyber Montenegro. It discusses AI in warfare through an ethical lens stressing that autonomous systems must not operate without meaningful human control and accountability. It calls for transparency in the development and deployment of AI weapons clear responsibility for decisions made by autonomous systems and adherence to humanitarian law and moral principles linking AI use to the need for human oversight and ethical governance.
cse2_name: Advantages and Risks of Using Artificial Intelligence in the Media Sector
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse2_justif: This study was conducted by the NGO Media Council for Self-Regulation. It requires transparency in AI use including informing users about algorithmic recommendations and AI-assisted content (page 12). It mandates clear labelling of AI-generated content and disclosure to audiences (pages 33 38–39). Platforms must ensure explainability transparency and accountability of personalization systems (page 19).Considering  human oversight and determination AI implementation is defined as an editorial decision requiring designated human accountability (page 16). All AI-assisted content must undergo human review and approval (pages 17 and 38). Journalists and editors retain full responsibility for published content (page 38).
cse3_name: Manual for Reporting on Artificial Intelligence (Media Council for Self-Regulation Montenegro)
cse3_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse3_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse3_justif: The manual “Reporting on Artificial Intelligence” published by the CSO Media Council for Self-Regulation embeds fairness transparency and human oversight throughout. It requires attention to dignity privacy equality and justice (page 11) and establishes “Requirement #5 Diversity non-discrimination and fairness” (page 55). It defines explainability through transparency and traceability (page 55) and calls for verifiability (page 11). It also sets “Requirement #1 Human agency and oversight” (page 55) and stresses that humans must ensure fairness accountability and ethics (page 116).

From September to December 2025 Teodora Kovacevic a final-year student of Information Technology will be working as their first intern. cse1_justif: The UNICEF “Tinkering with Tech” initiative led by UNICEF Montenegro aims to enhance children’s digital skills creativity problem-solving abilities and understanding of emerging technologies through hands-on play-based learning. The initiative introduces students and teachers in Montenegro to coding design thinking and responsible technology use promoting curiosity collaboration and innovation. From 2025 AI literacy was added further strengthening the initiative’s contribution to digital transformation in education and preparing young people for the digital future. Milutin Pavicevic CEO of the Montenegrin AI Association (MAIA) spoke about the importance and challenges of applying AI in teaching and the need to prepare teachers and the education system for these changes. cse1_name: International Conference 'Law and Security in the Digital World - The Impact of Artificial Intelligence'
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The international conference “Law and Security in the Digital World – The Impact of Artificial Intelligence” organized by the NGO Association of Security Managers and the Faculty of Law of the University of Montenegro focused on the impact of artificial intelligence on law and security. The working panels brought together experts and leaders from the fields of law and security who through five discussions addressed issues of international and national legislation on AI application whether AI is an ally or a threat in protecting critical infrastructure and topics of modern forensics the challenges of the digital age cyberspace and security in the era of digitalization.
cse2_name: Legal Challenges of Artificial Intelligence in Cybersecurity – with a focus on copyright protection
cse2_drive: The evidence does not consist of a document.
cse2_type: Other
cse2_contributions: Safety and Security
cse2_justif: The article “Legal Challenges of Artificial Intelligence in Cybersecurity with a Focus on Copyright Protection” published by the NGO Women4Cyber Montenegro analyzes the use of artificial intelligence in cybersecurity and the challenges that arise in this area. It points out that AI brings numerous advantages but also serious risks in cybersecurity stressing the importance of ethical and responsible application. Special attention is given to copyright protection and questions of authorship of AI-generated works as well as the need to clearly define responsibility and ownership. The article underlines the importance of aligning national legislation with EU legal standards and practices.
cse3_name: Countering Disinformation while Protecting Freedom of Expression
cse3_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse3_justif: Analytical report “Countering Disinformation while Protecting Freedom of Expression” by NGO Center for Democratic Transition (CDT) examines how emerging technologies including AI contribute to the creation and dissemination of false or manipulative content. AI-generated disinformation is discussed in Chapter I (pages 9–11). The need for transparent regulation institutional safeguards and media literacy is elaborated in Chapter II (pages 12–16). Further guidance and steps for strengthening national responses to digital and AI-driven disinformation are outlined in Chapter V (pages 24–27). Recommendations related to alignment with EU standards and public education on digital and AI-enabled disinformation are outlined in Chapter VI(page 28).
cse4_name: Montenegro facing a challenge – How to protect yourself from AI-generated false information?
cse4_drive: The evidence does not consist of a document.
cse4_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse4_contributions: AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse4_justif: Montenegro Media Institute’s article “Montenegro facing a challenge – How to protect yourself from AI-generated false information?” explains how AI misuse already happens in Montenegro gives local examples of manipulated interviews and fake ads defines deepfake and notes EU measures (Digital Services Act AI Act). It stresses media literacy education and training and the need for mechanisms to prevent the spread of false information highlighting risks to public trust and cybercrime reporting.

cse1_name: Trusted Artificial Intelligence: A Lever for Change in Favor of Accelerated Development in Morocco
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_justif: AI Movement a non-profit participated in a reflection session hosted by the Royal Institute for Strategic Studies (IRES) a public entity. The reflection session was titled 'Trustworthy AI: A level of change in favor of accelerated development in Morocco and aimed to develop recommendations for a National Strategy (pp. 5-6). One of the key recommendations supports the establishment of AI steering committees in the public and private sectors overseeing the integration of among other principles ethics and transparency (p. 29).
cse2_name: Cloud Crypto and Smart Contracts: What legal framework and what opportunities for Morocco?
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Environmental Impact
cse2_justif: AUSIM publishes a series of magazines and reports. This edition featured a discussion with the Minister of Digital Transition and Administrative Reform who emphasized the need for a governance model for AI. This model should ensure equitable and inclusive access while addressing disparities to prevent bias and exclusion. Additionally models and algorithms must be designed to be useful inclusive cost-effective and safe. Collaboration among international stakeholders is essential to align AI research with ethical principles and ensure that governance promotes fairness and transparency. The governance of AI should also prioritize sustainability ensuring that the training processes do not have a negative impact on the environment. (p. 47)
cse3_name: Adopting AI in your company: a strategic plan
cse3_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse3_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse3_justif: AUSIM released the 'Adopting AI in your company: a strategic plan' in partnership with AI Crafters. The report highlights best practices for integrating AI into companies. One of the first steps in automation is integrating AI models into existing information systems ensuring their transparent implementation.p. 45 The report recommends using the AI Transformation Framework to automate their processes while maintaining human control over critical decisions p.78 The reports also emphasise the need to establish an ethics Committee exemplified by Microsoft's committee established to oversee the development and use of its AI solutions particularly to avoid bias in algorithms and ensure that AI is deployed in a fair and transparent mannerp.104

These discussions addressed the necessity of endorsing cultural diversity in the context of AI particularly for Africans. By inviting high-level government entities and parliamentary members to the forum AI Movement raised awareness among decision-makers and tried to influence their AI policies.

Throughout this training 80 women participants from 28 African countries engaged in a program designed to foster innovation leadership and collaboration. Projects' themes include: inclusion agriculture health and education; topics emerging for local needs. This initiative addresses therefore gender equality by empowering African Women in the context of AI in addressing local needs.

cse1_name: Public Seminar: Youth and Women in the Artificial Intelligence Ecosystem in Mozambique
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The seminar organized by the organization Mozambique Inclusion (Inclusão Moçambique) focused on promoting the participation of young people and women in Mozambique’s AI ecosystem. It created a platform for dialogue on opportunities challenges and gender inclusion in emerging technologies while also highlighting the role of youth in shaping equitable digital futures.

cse1_name: International Fact-Checking Day: Combating disinformation is everyone’s responsibility!
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The initiative addresses AI-facilitated misinformation and violence by raising public awareness of how emerging technologies including artificial intelligence can be misused to spread false or manipulated content. It emphasizes that AI tools while capable of supporting verification are increasingly used to amplify disinformation particularly during elections and calls for collective responsibility to counter these risks.
cse2_name: Artificial Intelligence Could Be a Threat to Mozambique’s History
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse2_justif: The initiative contributes to addressing AI-facilitated misinformation by highlighting the risk of AI generating false historical narratives about Mozambique which can mislead the public especially younger generations.
