cse1_name: Myanmar’s repressive use of AI to counter “terrorism”
cse1_link: The Wayback Machine website returns an error for the following link: 'https://web.archive.org/web/20251216075728/https://humanrightsmyanmar.org/myanmars-repressive-use-of-ai-to-counter-terrorism/' please see the original link:https://humanrightsmyanmar.org/myanmars-repressive-use-of-ai-to-counter-terrorism/
cse1_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse1_contributions: Safety and Security;  Impact Assessments;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse1_justif: The initiative addresses Safety and Security Impact Assessments by examining how  military weaponises AI under the guise of counter-terrorism to target civilians conduct surveillance and enable arbitrary arrests and torture. Sections on the AI-powered dictatorship and AI’s impact on digital rights (pp. 4–27) function as risk assessments linking AI systems to violations of life liberty and privacy. It also tackles AI-facilitated misinformation and violence (pp. 28–44) analysing how algorithmic platforms like Meta and Telegram amplify hate doxing and propaganda that fuel offline attacks and ethnic persecution.
cse2_name: Risks of creative AI in Myanmar
cse2_link: The Wayback Machine website returns an error for the following link: 'https://web.archive.org/web/20251022145159/https://humanrightsmyanmar.org/risks-of-creative-ai-in-myanmar/' due to see the original link:https://humanrightsmyanmar.org/risks-of-creative-ai-in-myanmar/
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Impact Assessments
cse2_justif: The report highlights key trust and safety risks related to AI actors particularly in the chapter on Heightened Due Diligence (p. 4). It notes that Myanmar’s experience shows technology companies often fail to conduct adequate due diligence before deploying new digital applications especially in the Global South. It stresses that given the significant risks posed by creative AI companies should consult independent experts to assess potential human rights harms. The Recommendations chapter (p. 5) calls for heightened due diligence in conflict zones in line with the UNGPs and urges stronger international norms including binding AI impact assessments victim remedies and UN monitoring mechanisms.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: Computer Association of Nepal(CAN) proposes 10-point recommendations for Nepal’s AI policy
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse1_justif: Computer Association of Nepal (CAN) in the recommendation appealed for  need for a robust legal framework and strong data protection policies to safeguard AI-related data and set clear guidelines on data management including aspects such as data cleansing safety and privacy. The organization also proposed the development of a dictionary containing definitions of technical terms such as AI machine learning and data privacy ensuring clarity for policymakers and stakeholders which can be an approach towards sustainability.
cse2_name: UNESCO's Unveiling the Intricacies of AI Governance in Nepal
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse2_justif: The multistakeholder dialogue co-organized with Digital Rights Nepal addressed AI governance through discussions on equality non-discrimination transparency and accessibility. Panels on “AI Governance and Legal Framework” and “AI Regulation in Nepal” examined regulatory needs ethical safeguards and inclusion of marginalized communities and persons with disabilities. The event also reinforced UNESCO’s 2021 Recommendation on the Ethics of AI promoting fairness and responsible AI development in Nepal.
cse3_name: A Case for Nepal's Al Diplomacy: Artificial Intelligence and Diplomacy
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse3_justif: Extract from Page 36 (Drive full document) reads 'Addressing Bias and Fairness that includes promoting policies for the development and use of fair and unbiased AI systems—that incorporates addressing data bias algorithmic bias and ensuring that AI systems do not perpetuate or exacerbate existing inequalities.' Extract from page 48 reads 'By participating in the development of international AI protocols concerning data sharing privacy and transparency Nepal can help ensure that emerging norms are equitable and consider the interests of smaller nations'

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Computer Association of Nepal (CAN) recommends changes to National AI Policy 2025
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_justif: The initiative contributes to Cultural and Linguistic Diversity by recommending that AI technologies be tailored to Nepal’s linguistic context. The concept paper calls for AI systems focused on Nepali and regional language models promoting inclusion of speakers of diverse local languages. By advocating AI solutions adapted to Nepal’s multilingual society the initiative seeks to reduce exclusion of non-English-speaking populations and ensure equitable access to AI benefits
cse2_name: Women In Data
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Gender Equality
cse2_justif: The panel discussion focused on the historical underrepresentation of women in STEM fields and how the growing popularity of Artificial intelligence exacerbates the issue. The discussions also spotlighted the intersectionality in fostering inclusion for women in the field.
cse3_name: Nepal National Summit on Artificial Intelligence 2024
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_justif: The Summit Report: Nepal National Summit on Artificial Intelligence 2024 addresses Gender Equality and Cultural and Linguistic Diversity by explicitly tackling AI bias and inclusivity. On p.16 Ms. Aakriti Kharel stressed prioritizing women in science and computer education and embedding human rights in AI policy. On p.18 (Section 3: Bias and Inclusivity) the report calls for addressing AI bias promoting women’s inclusion and enhancing accessibility in public services including language recognition in government offices.
cse4_name: Safeguarding Futures: Exploring the Impacts of Generative AI onChild Online Protection in Nepal
cse4_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse4_contributions: Children’s Rights
cse4_justif: Safeguarding Futures: Exploring the Impacts of Generative AI on Child Online Protection in Nepal (ChildSafeNet Sept 2024) addresses Children’s Rights by analysing how Generative AI affects child safety online. Chapter 4 (pp.27–33) details risks such as cyberbullying CSAM/OCSEA privacy breaches biased content and psychological harm. Chapter 5 (pp.34–39) reviews gaps in national laws and policies and Chapter 6 (p.42) provides recommendations for child-centred AI regulation and safety-by-design measures.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: AI Usage and Practice in Nepal
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Safety and Security
cse1_justif: Computer Association Nepal (CAN) is an established  autonomous body working on the development of ICT. Before the National AI Policy was approved by the government  CAN submitted  recommendations to the government to revise the policy. The key recommendation included revision on Data Management Policies where CAN said  development of policies should clearly outline data governance including data cleansing safety and privacy measures. It also suggested Legal Framework and Data Protection and cross border data sharing.
cse2_name: KTM Journathon: Tackle Misinformation and Disinformation Using AI
cse2_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse2_contributions: AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Safety and Security
cse2_justif: The Nepal Investigative Multimedia Journalism Network organized the KTM Journathon (Feb 18–22 2024) to develop AI-based solutions to combat misinformation and disinformation. The press release explicitly states the event aimed to “tackle misinformation and disinformation using AI” and supported the creation of tools such as Khoj AI (verification extension) and Khop Board (chatbot providing accurate vaccine information) directly addressing AI-facilitated information harms and digital safety.
cse3_name: Future of Work in Nepal: Conference on Artificial Intelligence
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse3_justif: The sessions at the conference featured presentations from tech experts on combating misinformation and disinformation. At the conference the  Ministry of Education Science and Technology (MoEST) also emphasized on importance of ethical considerations and data privacy in the development and implementation of AI technologies.
cse4_name: A Comprehensive Study on Artificial Intelligence DigitalInfrastructure and Data Policies with Recommendations for Policy to Strengthen AI Ecosystem in Nepal
cse4_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse4_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse4_justif: The report recommends the government must take proactive measures to combat AI-generated misinformation by implementing stricter regulations and promoting public awareness. It suggests that Nepal should enhance its regulatory framework for AI to safeguard data security and privacy with particular attention to addressing challenges such as data bias and misuse. The report reads It is also crucial to update and strengthen data privacy laws and security measures to protect personal information. Facet Technology a research firm collaborated closely with Data for Development The Asia Foundation along with UKAID to prepare this report.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: Response from Amnesty International and Bits of Freedom to the Call for input on AI systems for risk assessment of criminal offences
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse1_justif: This input from Amnesty and Bits of Freedom addresses Fairness & Non-Discrimination as its core theme arguing that discriminatory 'criminal risk assessment' is a 'real problem' (p. 3)  not a hypothetical one. It cites numerous government scandals involving racial and socioeconomic bias (p. 3) and insists that any discriminatory system is prohibited and must be taken offline (p. 4) . On Transparency & Explainability the document focuses on governance criticising the lack of 'independent oversight' (p. 4) and legal clarity on which AI systems are covered by the law (p. 3). It explicitly demands that governments act 'carefully and transparently' (p. 5). It provides a strong critique of Human Oversight warning against its symbolic gesture.
cse2_name: ALLAI Academy
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse2_justif: ALLAI is a Dutch non-profit foundation based in Amsterdam. It engages with a wide range of stakeholders including policymakers social partners consumers and both public and private sector actors. Among its various initiatives ALLAI has launched the ALLAI Academy which offers training and workshops on topics such as AI fairness trustworthy AI and the EU AI Act.
cse3_name: Algo-Lit project Waag Futurelab
cse3_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse3_justif: The project’s core mission as described on its webpage is to make “the use of algorithms more transparent to citizens.” It focuses on helping both professionals and citizens understand algorithmic decision-making processes and on strengthening citizens’ right to explanations about high-risk algorithms. It addresses transparency and explainability by co-creating methods and tools with practitioners developing a transparency toolkit that brings together existing explanation methods and clarifying citizens’ rights and possible actions when affected by algorithmic systems. In addition the project conducts surveys among digital inclusion workers generating empirical insights into knowledge gaps and practical challenges.
cse4_name: The 5-star model for transparent algorithms
cse4_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse4_contributions: Transparency and Explainability
cse4_justif: The project is a collaboration between the Open State Foundation and The Green Land who jointly received the grant to further develop the 5-star model. They are funded by a grant from the 'maatschappelijke coalitie Over Informatie Gesproken. The project's title is 'The 5-Star Model for Transparent Algorithms'. The whitepaper's summary explicitly introduces it as 'a framework for step-by-step realisation of transparency'. The model is designed to make transparency 'concrete manageable and secure' by offering five progressive levels from 'Published' to 'Open'.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Input for digital inclusion
cse1_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse1_justif: leder(in) is a national-level Dutch civil society organisation working on inclusion and diversity with a focus on disability rights and accessibility and actively participates in public policy processes. Ahead of a parliamentary debate on Digital Inclusion (January 23 2025) Ieder(in) representing the Digital Inclusion User Network- a coalition advocating for the rights of people with disabilities and other groups addresses disability inclusion in AI and digital accessibility. It urges Parliament to ensure that AI applications are inclusive accessible and developed with a “Design for All” approach that prevents exclusion of people with disabilities women people of colour and the elderly.(page 2)
cse2_name: Facebook algorithm engages in prohibited gender discrimination
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Gender Equality
cse2_justif: Following a joint investigation by Global Witness and Dutch women’s rights organisations Global Witness and Bureau Clara Wichmann filed a complaint against Meta for discriminatory practices in its job advertisements. The Netherlands Institute for Human Rights concluded that Meta’s job-ad delivery system amounted to prohibited gender discrimination. This marks the first time in Europe that an official human rights body has found a social media algorithm guilty of gender discrimination setting an important precedent for greater accountability inclusion and diversity in AI systems.
cse3_name: DIVERSIFAIR
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_contributions: Gender Equality;  Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse3_justif: The DiversifAIR project tackles bias through an intersectional approach which inadvertently touches all four indicators. It examines how different forms of discrimination overlap affecting individuals in complex ways. The project contributes to gender equality by partnering with groups like Women in AI providing training to detect bias and influencing policy to prevent gender-based stereotyping in AI algorithms. It promotes cultural and linguistic diversity by advocating for changes in data practices and promoting the inclusion of diverse perspectives. While children rights and rights of persons with disabilities is not directly mentioned but the project focusses on marginalised voices.
cse4_name: DMIS - Shaping Ethical AI for Social Impact
cse4_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse4_contributions: Gender Equality;  Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse4_justif: The summit advances inclusivity by encouraging discussions on ethical AI for marginalised groups. The resulting Haarlem Declaration commits to reducing bias and discrimination in AI systems. Across its various sessions the summit promotes cultural and linguistic diversity by encouraging the inclusion of Global South perspectives and underrepresented communities and languages in AI narratives and policymaking (pp. 8 9 24). It also references the MIT “Gender Shades” study as a key resource for advancing human-centred and ethical AI (p. 6).
cse5_justif: Gender equality is promoted by showing how harmful sexual behaviours are shaped by misogyny and hegemonic masculinity understood as norms of male dominance. It promotes gender-transformative consent-based education and intersectional approaches responsive to diverse identities including gender sexuality (SOGIESC) and ability (Section 5.1.2 “Gender” pp. 7 30; Section 5.3.4 pp. 8 42; Section 7.1.4 pp. 10 46).It advances children’s rights by framing HSBs as a child-rights and safety issue that harms children’s well-being and development and by prioritising child-focused non-judgemental and trauma-informed responses that protect children’s safety dignity and rights to age-appropriate sexual information (p 5-6 10 sec 7.1)

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: AI and work
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: The SER  a statutory advisory body that brings together representatives of employers trade unions and independent experts to advise the Dutch government and parliament on social and economic policy was asked to provide guidance on the opportunities of artificial intelligence (AI) to increase labor productivity as well as the opportunities and risks for job quality and the potential impact on employment and income inequality. In response it drafted an advisory report 'AI and work' and presented the findings to the House of Representatives' Digital Affairs Committee. It emphasised the establishment of  'Decent Work' as a non-negotiable standard for AI policy.(p 105).
cse2_name: Facebook algorithm engages in prohibited gender discrimination
cse2_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse2_contributions: Labour Protections
cse2_justif: The complaint filed by Bureau Clara Wichmann and Global Witness directly addresses labour protections by challenging Meta’s job-advertising algorithm which was found by the Netherlands Institute for Human Rights to produce prohibited indirect gender discrimination limiting equal access to employment opportunities. https://web.archive.org/web/20251216193830/https://clara-wichmann.nl/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Oordeel-180225-College-voor-de-Rechten-van-de-Mens-.pdf. The decision (2025-17 sections “Facts of the case” and “Assessment”) details how AI-driven ad personalisation reinforced stereotypes (e.g. mechanics primarily shown to men receptionists to women) illustrating a concrete breach of Dutch equal-treatment law.
cse3_name: CNV AI Academy
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_contributions: AI Literacy;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives
cse3_justif: CNV the Dutch Trade Union Federation has the CNV AI Academy which is an online/blended training programme for (future) school-leaders in secondary education. It is meant for secondary school leaders offering three modules: AI Rising Star (foundational AI knowledge) AI Go-To Person (internal support and guidance) and The Right AI Moves (strategic AI adoption in schools). It addresses AI literacy by building participants’ understanding of AI concepts and applications and reskilling/upskilling by developing leadership and organisational capacity to implement AI safely
cse4_name: Reshaping Work 2024 conference
cse4_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse4_contributions: Labour Protections;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives
cse4_justif: The Reshaping Work 2024 programme centers on work-related impacts of AI with explicit sessions on “AI Skills and Labor Relations” (Forum 8) panels “AI in HR” and “AI in Digital Platforms” and multi-stakeholder “Think Like…” roundtables. These sessions directly address worker impacts collective bargaining and policy responses.
cse5_name: The impact of artificial intelligence (AI) in the workplace
cse5_justif: FNV is a federation that acts as an umbrella organisation for various sector-specific unions. In March 2024 it issued a public call for evidence and a summary of initial findings from sector members (Call Centers/Logistics). It functioned as a (Hotline) to gather the data needed for policy influence. The report summary discusses how the FNV identifies possible job cuts as an issue that directly threatens job security particularly in sectors like ICT and call centers where technology companies are already linking the reduction of positions to the introduction of AI. By documenting these developments the FNV justifies the need for labor protections that address the risk of algorithms taking over entire roles and functions.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: Position paper Bits of Freedom
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Consumer Protection;  Access to Redress and Remedy
cse1_justif: The document is a 'Position paper' submitted by Bits of Freedom for a 'roundtable discussion' held by the Dutch Parliament's Digital Affairs Committee. Access to Remedy and Redress is the paper's primary focus. Bits of Freedom argues that the regulator (ACM) is failing to inform end users about their right to 'file a complaint'  calling this a 'crucial aspect of End-user protection'(p.1). The first principle stated is 'End users must be central' (p. 1 ). It criticises the regulator's website for being too 'focused on businesses' instead of the end users who should benefit. It addresses the core safety risks the law must solve including 'data hunger and tracking-based advertising' and 'sensationalist algorithms and manipulative design'.
cse2_name: Bits of Freedom vs Meta
cse2_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Consumer Protection;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse2_justif: Bits of Freedom is suing Meta citing the Digital Services Act (DSA) to demand that users be allowed to set a non-profiling chronological feed as their default. This case addresses data privacy by challenging Meta's constant monitoring and profiling of user behavior seeks to enhance consumer protection by enforcing a user's right to genuinely control their own timeline instead of being forced into Meta's preferred algorithmic feed and tackles AI-related misinformation by aiming to limit the power of recommendation algorithms that can be 'misused to covertly influence voters.'
cse3_name: Incentive Scheme 2025: Media-wise use of AI
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_contributions: Safety and Security;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse3_justif: This initiative Netwerk Mediawijsheid’s Stimuleringsregeling 2025: Mediawijze inzet van AI is a grant scheme designed to support Dutch projects to make Netherlands media literate. Netwerk Mediawijsheid (Media Literacy Network) is the national expertise center in the Netherlands dedicated to helping everyone become 'media-wise.' It is a massive collaboration of over 1200 partners including libraries schools cultural institutions media etc. The programme aims to fund initiatives that explore or apply AI with attention to societal impact focusing on four major challenges. Out of these four the grant focusses on resilience against disinformation and reflection on social dimensions such as safety and autonomy when using AI.
cse4_name: Social Research Agenda on AI by Waag Futurelab
cse4_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse4_contributions: AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Data Protection and Privacy
cse4_justif: The Social Research Agenda on AI (2024) developed by Waag Futurelab and the Dutch AI Coalition addresses data protection privacy safety and security and AI-facilitated misinformation and violence through several themes and recommendations. This initiative surveyed the Dutch population on AI research priorities directly addressing key concerns. AI-facilitated misinformation emerged as the #1 priority with 58% citing 'Fake news fake photos and polarisation' (p. 2 27) due to worries about deep fakes (p. 3).  Data protection and privacy was the #3 priority (33%) via the theme 'Supervision of people and freedom' (p. 3 30) reflecting public concern over data control and misuse (p. 3 23).
cse5_name: Police use unlawful incorrect and outdated information on a large scale for investigations
cse5_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Data Sharing and Access;  Safety and Security
cse5_justif: Follow the Money an investigative journalism platform published a report which raised concerns about the police’s reliance on automated data analysis which has led to the buildup of large databases containing reportedly unlawfully obtained and outdated information. The investigation reveals that the Dutch police extensively collect and analyse vast amounts of personal data using automated tools like AI. These analyses increasingly drive law enforcement and public order enforcement. Despite legal obligations to delete such data the police have failed to do so for years exposing citizens to potential misuse
cse6_name: EU Code of Conduct for Data Sharing in the Social Economy
cse6_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse6_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Data Sharing and Access
cse6_justif: This is a set of principles and practical guidelines co-created by Commons Network and Waag Futurelab to help social economy organizations (like cooperatives) share data in a way that aligns with their values. Data Sharing and Access is the document's central topic. It is a toolkit to help social economy organizations share data. Chapter 2 'Guidance on Data Management & Sharing' (p. 20) provides governance models (p. 26) and practical 'Data Sharing Agreement Checklists' (p. 30).The Code embeds privacy in its core values. The principle of 'Self-determination' (p. 15) states data subjects must have control and 'Data Sufficiency' (p. 18) acts as a data minimisation principle to 'avoid the unnecessary collection storage and processing'.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: Explainable Al: building trust through understanding
cse1_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse1_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse1_justif: AI Forum NZ (an NGO) published a whitepaper on AI explainability with a particular focus on machine learning (p. 8). The paper discusses the relationship between model performance and explainability and highlights key benefits of interpretability including improved error detection stronger user understanding (pp. 9–11) bias reduction and increased trust. It also provides guidance on transparency the role of human decision-making across the AI lifecycle and responsibility (p. 7) and outlines a developer- and user-oriented conception of fairness (pp. 3 7 18).
cse2_name: Guidelines for the best-practice use of generative artificial intelligence in research in Aotearoa New Zealand
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse2_justif: Royal Society Te Aparangi is an independent NGO serving NZ’s research sector. One of the Society’s parliamentary remits is to provide infrastructure and other support for the professional needs and development of science technology and humanities researchers including research practice guidance. This guidance released in June 2025 is to help researchers leverage GenAI’s innovative benefits while remaining aware of ethical risks; their guidelines advocate transparent disclosure of GenAI use and limitations (Pg 5) mandate active mitigation of bias to promote inclusion (Pg 5 Pg 7) and ensure full accountability and oversight rests with human researchers (Pg 5) not AI systems.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Netsafe submission to the Parliamentary Inquiry into the harm young New Zealanders encounter online and the roles that Government business and society should play in addressing those harms
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: Children’s Rights;  Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse1_justif: Netsafe is NZ’s independent non-profit online safety organisation focused on advancing digital safety protecting the rights of NZ’ers including children and young people and promoting responsible digital citizenship. In this submission Netsafe highlights the cases of AI-created deepfake imagery noting NZ’s laws do not adequately address synthetic sexual content and non-consensual use involving children. The submission urges platform accountability rejecting blanket bans and promoting risk-based rights-respecting approaches that consider children’s participation wellbeing and dignity online. (Pg 14).
cse2_name: AI with Eteroa
cse2_drive: This is a webpage and not a document.
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse2_justif: AI with Eteroa is a free online learning experience designed to empower Pacific communities with the skills knowledge and confidence to navigate the AI wave. The free course offers 4 modules; Understanding AI Machine Learning Understanding Data Prompting.
cse3_name: AI and Accessibility: Keeping it human
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_justif: Access Advisors is an independent nationwide pan-disability consultancy and a for purpose organisation. Their mission is to help  help Aotearoa New Zealand become more digitally inclusive. The blogs on their webpage reflects their thoughts and work. This blog by the org argues that while AI can help charities and organisations streamline work and improve accessibility it must remain a tool that supports rather than replaces human expertise and empathy. It stresses that AI cannot fully understand emotional or contextual nuance (for example in writing alt text) and highlights serious concerns around data sovereignty security and biased training data particularly for disabled people.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: A.I For Good: 10 perspectives on artificial intelligence
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  Labour Protections
cse1_justif: Public Service Association (PSA) NZ published 'AI for Good' (2025) with perspectives on labour protection and upskilling advocating for labour rights. One of the perspectives advocates for workers rights: 'involving the people affected the workers in determining the inputs at both the production and deployment stages will help to effect this shift to human centered AI (Not by Chance; but by Choice Pg 8-9). Also advocates for 'intentionally aimed at undermining organised labour and worker voice must be prohibited and both the producers and deployers of such products should be penalised' p.8-9. Another perspective calls for a Just Transition where upskilling should be a right and Maori data sovereignty must be respected (p25- 27).
cse2_name: Artificial Intelligence in the Workplace
cse2_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse2_contributions: Labour Protections
cse2_justif: NZ Council of Trade Union's report outlines practical strategies and principles for labour protection in the face of workplace AI adoption. It urges unions to leverage existing laws (like the Employment Relations Act Health and Safety at Work Act Privacy Act Human Rights Act) (Pg 8) and collective bargaining to protect workers. Key recommendations include worker voice in AI decisions impact assessments for AI tools (pg 10) robust health and safety protocols (pg 17) clear data privacy and protection guidelines (Pg 20) limits on surveillance (pg 4) and model clauses for collective agreements that ensure fair job quality (Pg 5) prevent job loss (pg 18) and uphold data sovereignty and the right to disconnect (pg 6).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: Aotearoa AI Summit 2025
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The Aotearoa AI Summit 2025 organised by AI Forum NZ and NZ Tech is a one-day national event uniting industry leaders researchers policymakers and innovators to discuss AI ethics responsible innovation and real-world applications across sectors like healthcare finance agriculture and government through keynotes panels and workshops. In the 2025 summit there were sessions on Maori data sovereignty data protection AI governance. The Summit aims to share space for cutting-edge research success stories and future trends shaping the AI landscape in Aotearoa.
cse2_name: How to improve the Aotearoa New Zealand Code of Practice forOnline Safety and Harms?
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Safety and Security;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse2_justif: The report presented by NZ Human Rights Commision presents findings from a three-month Independent Accountability Group that examined selected human rights and Te Tiriti o Waitangi issues arising from the Aotearoa New Zealand Code of Practice for Online Safety and Harms. (Pg 2) It uses concrete examples of online abuse including a detailed review of serious harm directed at former Prime Minister Dame Jacinda Ardern during the election campaign on X (Pg 3) and Facebook to inform its analysis of how the Code addresses online safety and harms in Aotearoa New Zealand. (Pg 9).
cse3_name: AI and Online Safety: Emerging Risks and Opportunities
cse3_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse3_justif: Netsafe and AI Asia Pacific Institute published a paper to analyse & provide insights into the implications of AI on online safety. The paper identifies key trends challenges and strategies for addressing online harms including AI-facilitated issues. The objective is to offer insights and observations for policymakers industry stakeholders and educators to foster a safer digital environment. Online harms explored in this paper are limited to those affecting individuals and society from online content and digital comms in the following categories: child sexualexploitation and abuse violent and graphic content extremism harm to health and well-being indecent and obscene content hate & discrimination cyberbullying (Overview Pg 7).

It is an association of international NGOs working in the humanitarian field in Niger.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: Fact-Checking & Artificial Intelligence (AI): Youth in Action
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_justif: The Tillabéri training (“Fact-checking & Artificial Intelligence: Youth in Action” 23–25 Sept 2025) addresses AI-facilitated misinformation and violence by equipping youth leaders with practical fact-checking skills (source verification rumour deconstruction critical analysis) and explaining how Artificial Intelligence tools can help detect fake news (methods limits opportunities). It emphasises combining critical thinking with technology to strengthen community resilience to conspiracy narratives and to protect public space from information manipulation.

cse1_name: AI Integration Training for Educators under the Nigeria AI Collective Portfolio
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse1_justif: The initiative addresses the selected areas by focusing on AI literacy and curriculum reform.Human Oversight and Determination: By training educators the initiative ensures future professionals (journalists communicators) have the critical skills to scrutinize AI recognize risks and maintain human control over content creation auditing and decision-making processes preventing blind reliance on automation.Transparency and Explainability: The workshop's content emphasizes teaching strategies for embedding algorithmic bias and data privacy into curricula. This directly builds the capacity of future workers to understand how AI makes decisions thereby demanding greater transparency and explainability from the systems they use.

cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_contributions: AI Literacy;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives
cse1_justif: The Brain Builders Youth Development Initiative (BBYDI) advances AI literacy by championing a nationwide review/validation of an AI literacy curriculum to improve what learners are taught about AI and how it is taught. It supports reskilling/upskilling by linking the curriculum update to implementation steps such as educator engagement and planned rollout helping build practical AI competencies among teachers and students for future work and learning in AI-enabled environments.

cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.

cse1_justif: The initiative promotes data sharing and access by PLSI’s revamped civic technology platform Value for Money 3.0 platform to make public finance and service delivery information easily accessible to citizens enabling faster petition submission and engagement with anti-corruption agencies. It also addresses data protection and privacy by emphasising secure data handling compliance with privacy laws and the need for national AI governance and ethical standards discussed during the focused group session.

cse2_name: Surveillance Technologies at European Borders Assessment on NorthMacedonia
cse2_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse2_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse2_justif: Civil society organizations have been denouncing the dangers of the use of new technologies and Artificial Intelligence in the field of migration and border control including the deployment of intrusive surveillance technology and the collection of biometric data from people on the move. The lack of transparency and regulation surrounding these processes and their impact results in a lack of accountability on the part of the authorities tech companies as well as public research institutions as it poses severe difficulties in the monitoring of likely violations of human rights.
cse3_name: Human Rights Protection in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse3_justif: Overall objective of the project „Human Rights Protection in the Age of Artificial Intelligence“ is to shape AI policies and regulatory frameworks that uphold human rights online. Specific objectives: Raise public awareness of the challenges and risks imposed by adopting upcoming strategic framework and related legislation related to AI to human rights online. Support policy development in North Macedonia in the area of protection of online privacy and other human rights online. Target groups :Citizens including marginalized groups: Civil society organizations and Media
cse4_name: Analytical report on the Foreign Information Manipulation and Interference (FIMI) in the Republic of North Macedonia
cse4_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse4_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse4_justif: The research report writes that there has been some disinformation based on generative AI circulating on Macedonian social media most of it consists of reshared or reposted content from foreign politicians or leaders.This is largely because AI-generated disinformation in North Macedonia has not yet reached the advanced stage seen in other countries. A significant barrier to this is that most sophisticateddeepfake and AI-altered content is trained on vast datasets in native languages primarily English. In North Macedonia there has been virtually no targeting of politicians or public figures with thistechnology due to the lack of sufficient content required to produce realistic deepfakes that can pass the 'human-eye test.

cse5_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse5_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse5_justif: The initiative represents a research-based ethical standards to guide the creation implementation and use of AI systems. These standards emphasize balancing innovation with responsibility to safeguard human rights focusing on safety trustworthiness and accountability in automated processes.

cse1_justif: Gender equality links to the focus on AI bias and discrimination and to Ethics by Design: Building Inclusive and Non-invasive Technological Solutions promoting non-discrimination and warning tech can deepen inequalities. Children’s rights link to Cyber Shake -Youth dialogues: Why study when there is Artificial Intelligence? on pupils/students' AI use misuse detecting model written texts consequences in education and regulation. Rights of persons with disabilities link to Ethics by Design meeting the diverse needs of citizens and aiming to empower and include rather than marginalize plus Lightning Talk-on transparency and equitable .Full agenda here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/116_xtfIeiOXjpdDrAi11MuW0gX1ViShO/view?usp=drive_link
cse2_name: Ethics and bias in artificial intelligence
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse2_justif: The Republic of North Macedonia and neighboring countries face the challenge of adapting to the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence (AI). With ethical standards and the need to prevent bias global and European initiatives (such as the new EU Artificial Intelligence Act the AI-Act) are introducing strict rules for the responsible use of AI but there is mixed progress in the region. The document addresses and debates on several dimensions. On page 47 for example mentions that : 'Imported educational technologies often do not reflect the linguistic cultural and socio-economic realities of students in the region'

cse1_name: The Digital Transformation of the Parliament: Civil Society as a Key Partner in Building a Modern Parliament
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_justif: University professors civil society experts and digitalization experts from European countries debated in the Parliament of the Republic of North Macedonia regarding the Digital Transformation Strategy of the Parliament. In a panel discussion titled: 'Parliament 2.0 - Change Leadership Capacity and Inclusion' the participants shared their thoughts on a more effective digital strategy towards the goal of a more modern Parliament and foreign experts encouraged use of AI in the Parliament.
cse2_name: Open your AIs: Responsible Use of AI Tools by Youth Organisations
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: AI Literacy;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives
cse2_justif: The study session pursues three key objectives: helping youth organisations remain vigilant and responsible when integrating AI into their work; supporting them to become vocal advocates for AI ethics within their structures and among their beneficiaries and building knowledge to navigate and contribute to key policy processes like the EU AI Act and the Council of Europe’s AI Convention especially considering growing concerns around AI-driven discrimination.
cse3_name: Inclusiveness in the process of creating support programs and partnership with the business sector in the Municipality of Centar
cse3_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse3_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy
cse3_justif: Public consultations held to explore several aspect of the possibilities of cooperation with the business sector among companies in Municipality of Center including AI tools. The responses from the survey also provided information that in 2023 only one quarter used AI tools at work and 61% responded that they would like to attend a training on AI in 2024. The survey provides disaggregation by sector company size and so on (page 24-26). The public consultations served as an input for the Strategy for local economic development of the Municipality. https://www.centar.gov.mk/sites/default/files/alfa/doc/programi/strategija-ler-2024-2028.pdf
cse4_name: TRANSFORMATION OF EMPLOYMENT RELATIONS: ADDRESSING THE CHALLENGES OF AI AND DIGITAL PLATFORM WORKERS
cse4_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse4_contributions: Labour Protections;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy
cse4_justif: The publication of the Konrad Adenauer Foundation  jointly with the Institute for Democracy yielded a series of research papers that address the AI and the public administration. The minister for digital transformation also writes an article on: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCEAND THE EFFICIENCY OF THE MACEDONIAN PUBLIC ADMINISTRATION. Overall papers cover all three initiatives: Labour protection upskilling of the public administration and AI literacy.
cse5_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse5_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy
cse5_justif: This is a document prepared by the Westminster Foundation for Democracy (WFD) in July 2024 with contributions from international parliamentary experts and translated in Macedonian by their local office. It offers practical guidance for integrating artificial intelligence (AI) into parliamentary operations in North Macedonia with focus on ethical and human rights and capacity building like trainings on AI. (page 9-capacity building and education)page 72-provide instructions about trainings and education; page 75-Organize frequent artificial intelligence training programs for parliamentary officials and administrators.

cse1_justif: (page 2 - Gdrive link) Related to the introduction of AI-based solutions in the delivery of public services Metamorphosis conducted research on the impact of AI on human rights analyzing European and international regulations and ethical standards. The participant   emphasized that before applying AI in the public sector it is necessary to establish clear strategies and policies that will ensure that the technology does not have a negative impact on human rights especially in automated decision-making.
cse2_name: Effective Justice to Protect the Fundamental Freedoms and Privacy of People in the On-line Space
cse2_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Safety and Security;  Access to Redress and Remedy
cse2_justif: (page 1-GDrive link) North Macedonia is a signatory to the Convention for the Protection of Individuals with regard to Automatic Processing of Personal Data 108/81. The Law on Personal Data Protection was adopted in February 2020 to align the national legislation with the EU  (GDPR) as the highest legal standard for personal data. Although the Personal DataProtection Agency has a primary authority in this area to supervise the legality of the activities undertaken during the processing of personal data as well as the protection of the fundamental rights and freedoms of individuals regarding processing of their personal data judiciary and public prosecution have significant role in the protection of human rights in the online space.
cse3_name: From AI to Gamification: Transforming Education for the Digital Generation – Dig-Ed at REMA 2025
cse3_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse3_justif: Innovative program to create a workshop-based training methodology for primary and secondary schools integrating digital storytelling AI tools and game-based learning to make cybersecurity education dynamic and engaging. In late 2024 Dig-Ed developed supporting resources and issued an open call for teachers to participate in workshops.
cse4_name: Navigating AI’s Impact on Human Rights in a Digital Era at the 19th International e-Society.mk Conference
cse4_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse4_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Safety and Security
cse4_justif: The conference explores the artificial intelligence (AI) which opens unprecedented opportunities for innovation in various sectors while at the same time opening different questions on the protection of basic human rights. Achieving the right balance between innovation and accountability is essential to ensuring a digital landscape that includes addressing issues such as privacy bias discrimination and the ethical use of AI to protect human rights. The conference offered a possibility for networking with international experts.
cse5_name: Ethical standards for protecting human rights online in automated decision-making
cse5_contributions: AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Safety and Security;  Impact Assessments
cse5_justif: The document (in Macedonian) focuses on the areas selected. It emphasizes the importance of ensuring that AI systems are developed and used in ways that prioritize safety and do not compromise fundamental rights or public security (pages 13–14). Then it highlights the need for thorough impact assessments especially Data Protection Impact Assessments to evaluate how AI might affect privacy and human rights before these technologies are deployed (page 30). Also it mentions about the potential misuse of AI to spread misinformation or incite violence stressing the need for safeguards to prevent such outcomes (page 19).
cse6_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse6_contributions: Safety and Security;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Data Protection and Privacy
cse6_justif: A consortium of Macedonian CSO's  partnered with the government on “MKSafeNet” a project to establish the first National Safer Internet Centre. Backed by an EU Digital Europe grant and the Ministry of Digital Transformation MKSafeNet is creating an awareness hub school programs a youth panel and a helpline for reporting online abuse including guidelines about AI tools filters and related resources.

cse1_name: Webinar: Is your business ready for artificial intelligence?
cse1_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse1_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse1_justif: The workshop is created by the Confederation of Norwegian Enterprise (NHO) organization that operates as a non-profit. The webinar is meant for businesses and employers that want to adopt AI techniques responsibly with respect to their employees: p.2:'Build safe environments for experimentation' p.2 also mentions risks and compliance with laws and regulations. P.3 talks about transparency and trust labeling AI generated content and confidentiality. Another point addressed:'How do you avoid AI creating a sense of surveillance or loss of autonomy among employees? (impact on employees)'.P.4 brings up responsibility and consequences for AI mistakes and failures.

cse1_name: The  Consumer  Council's  input  to  the  Screen  Usage  Committee
cse1_type: Developing ethical guidelines principles or best practices.
cse1_justif: The activity is a policy submission to a governmental committee on children’s screen use led by the Norwegian Consumer Council a Norwegian CSO . It focuses on risks arising from AI-driven digital environments for minors including addictive design targeted advertising and AI-based “virtual companions.” The activity directly addresses Children’s Rights by advocating stronger protections precautionary approaches to AI in schools and regulatory safeguards against manipulation and exploitation (pp. 2-3).
cse2_name: Measuring prejudice in AI systems using Norwegian language and context
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Gender Equality;  Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse2_justif: Norwegian AI Ethics Association (NAIE) is advocating for developing Norway's own benchmark and guidelines for measuring and detecting biases in AI models. There has been multiple installments of an ongoing experiment where the group has been adapting some existing benchmarks to Norwegian language and realities p.4 (previously: https://naie.no/en/measuring-bias-in-ai-systems-using-norwegian-language-and-context-part-1/). They use indicators to measure LLM biasedness towards gender age religion etc. (p.7). The authors are hoping that this project can gain visibility and motivate further developments in AI bias diversity and inclusion guidelines and audits.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: NITO consultation submission  –  draft  new  law  on  artificial  intelligence  (AI  Act)
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Labour Protections;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives
cse1_justif: The submission by the Norwegian CSO NITO – The Norwegian Association of Engineers and Technologists addresses Labour Protections by emphasizing employee rights in AI deployment including privacy co-determination and a safe working environment and by requiring involvement of shop stewards in AI-related workplace processes (pp. 1-2). It also addresses Reskilling / Upskilling initiatives by calling for strengthened support for continuing education long-term funding of industry programmes and a comprehensive competence boost to ensure workers can adapt to AI-driven changes in the workplace (pp. 1 3).
cse2_name: Artificial intelligence in the workplace: A survey of professional groups affiliated with Unio
cse2_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse2_contributions: Labour Protections;  Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy
cse2_justif: The report presents findings from a research project on how AI is used and experienced at work by employees in various professions affiliated with trade union Unio. Chapter 5 addresses mostly all three points of this indicator e.g. p63-64 point out the importance and current lack of AI skills and knowledge training need for overall AI expertise and literacy. In terms of labour protection members and union representatives report limited involvement in decisions about AI use(e.g. p67) suggesting a lack of structured worker protection mechanisms in how AI is introduced and governed at workplaces which is pointed out again in conclusions chapter 7.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: NITO consultation submission  –  draft  new  law  on  artificial  intelligence  (AI  Act)
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Data Sharing and Access;  Data Protection and Privacy;  Impact Assessments
cse1_justif: The activity led by the Norwegian CSO NITO – The Norwegian Association of Engineers and Technologists is a consultation submission (30 September 2025) on Norway’s draft AI Act. It addresses Data Protection and Privacy by emphasizing employee privacy and safeguards in workplace AI use (p. 2) Data Sharing and Access through calls for clearer guidance on data use and transparency requirements (p. 3) and Impact Assessments by explicitly advocating fundamental rights impact assessments for high-risk AI systems including workplace-related impacts (pp 2 4).
cse2_name: Complains about Meta's use of personal content for AI training
cse2_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Data Sharing and Access;  Consumer Protection
cse2_justif: The litigation led by the Norwegian CSO Consumer Council in collaboration with NOYB challenges Meta’s use of user content to train AI models without a valid legal basis. Filed with the Norwegian Data Protection Authority and other EU regulators it addresses Data Protection and Privacy (unlawful processing of personal data) Data Sharing and Access (repurposing user-generated content for AI training) and Consumer Protection (manipulative opt-out mechanisms undermining informed consent).
cse3_name: Four proposals for new AI policy
cse3_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse3_contributions: Safety and Security;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence;  Impact Assessments
cse3_justif: The report with proposals is written by Langsikt - an independent non-profit think tank for longer-term policy offering steps forward in AI regulations and developments. A big part of these proposals is strengthening AI safety policies by establishing a Norwegian AI Security Institute (p.4):'The institute should evaluate advanced AI systems and advise authorities business and the population about risks and safe use.'...'necessary to identify challenges in advanced AI systems such as the risk of hallucinations erroneous decisions discrimination privacy violations misinformation and unpredictable behavior. We also need a body that can define security requirements for AI models including testing transparency and human verification..'

cse2_name: National Artificial Intelligence Policy 2025 Analysis by Digital Rights Foundation
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Cultural and Linguistic Diversity
cse2_justif: The initiative is an analysis of the Digital Rights Foundation of the National Artificial Intelligence Policy. On page 5 of the paper under the heading of objective concerns are raised on the terms used in the AI Policy like 'cultural identity''context-sensitive' and 'local narratives'. It is observed that such vague terms may allow broad interpretations which could lead to censorship and risk reinforcing interpretations that often silence voices challenging harmful norms of society (p.5).

cse2_name: Impact of Artificial Intelligence on Job Displacement in Pakistan’s Industrial Sector
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Reskilling and upskilling initiatives;  AI Literacy
cse2_justif: This paper examines the increasing impact of artificial intelligence (AI) on employment patterns in Pakistan’s major economic sectors. Sector-level vulnerabilities are noted as the research identifies new patterns in the use of AI including smart manufacturing precision farming automated warehousing and AI-based customer service. The paper suggests practical policy recommendations such as targeted reskilling programs education reforms vocational training expansion and digital inclusion policies.

cse1_name: Legislative trends in the regulation of artificial intelligence in Central America and the Dominican Republicin cooperation with IPANDETEC
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Human Oversight and Determination;  Fairness and Non Discrimination
cse1_justif: In the Central American region and the Dominican Republic there has been a legislative trend on the regulation of artificial intelligence since 2023. These bills have been presented in three specific countries: Costa Rica Panama and the Dominican Republic which are still under discussion in their respective legislatures. A model law proposed by the Latin American and Caribbean Parliament also promotes AI regulation. Yet progress is slow and data protection laws are also outdated to deal with the new challenges of AI. This analysis was developed in cooperation with IPANDETEC.

The event promoted training on cybersecurity and data sharing in the context of AI.

cse1_name: SOPAIA (Paraguayan Society of Artificial Intelligence) participation in a public parliamentary hearing
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_justif: During the June 2025 parliamentary hearing on the draft AI law  SOPAIA representative Luis Benitez raised concerns about approving the bill without addressing core issues such as transparency explainability. The intervention emphasised that Paraguay lacks a coherent digital governance strategy. This participation helped highlight critical gaps in the legislative proposal.
cse2_name: Landmark court ruling in Paraguay on access to information in facial recognition case
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Transparency and Explainability
cse2_justif: TEDIC supported a landmark access-to-information case seeking disclosure of the location and operation of facial recognition cameras in Asunción. The ruling did not address the merits but recognised that lower courts had misapplied access-to-information law reopening the case and strengthening transparency standards. While the ruling did not decide the substantive legality of FR it strengthens public scrutiny and accountability over AI-based surveillance systems.
cse3_name: AIkuaa a project to teach Guarani to Artificial Intelligence
cse3_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse3_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability
cse3_justif: Aikuaa a collaborative journalism and civic-education project by El Surti demystifies AI for the public through accessible culturally grounded explanations. Its work addresses algorithmic discrimination power asymmetries and the social impacts of AI promoting fairness and inclusion. The project also advances transparency by explaining how AI systems function how data and models shape outputs and what risks they pose. SurtiLab’s technical documentation further supports explainability by openly detailing the model dataset architecture and limitations.
cse4_name: IAfter: A space to interact and reflect on the uses of Artificial Intelligence
cse4_type: Litigation or legal action.
cse4_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse4_justif: TEDIC’s “IAfter” initiative creates a public space for dialogue and reflection on how AI impacts society rights and democratic life. Through open events interactive sessions and participatory discussions it raises awareness about the ethical and social implications of AI including fairness human oversight and determination and accountability. The initiative helps broaden public understanding of AI systems and encourages informed civic engagement on their deployment.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Feminisms and Technologies – Research Report by TEDIC (2025)
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_justif: TEDIC’s 2025 research Feminisms and Technologies by Montserrat Fois Verónica Villalba M. and Jazmín Sánchez analyzes how AI and algorithmic systems reproduce gender bias exclusion and violence in Latin America. Combining feminist theory and digital rights it proposes inclusive and intersectional approaches to AI governance contributing to public debate and knowledge production on responsible AI and gender equality in Paraguay and the region. In point 6.8 the study specifically addresses gender bias in AI and its algorithms (pp. 49-51).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Labour and Skills
cse1_name: Civil Society Advocacy and Declaration on Platform Work and Algorithmic Management in Paraguay (TEDIC / Fairwork-related initiative)
cse1_type: Public engagement & awareness: conferences public awareness events/campaigns public consultations.
cse1_justif: The initiative addresses labour protections in AI-mediated work by focusing on platform labour governed through algorithmic management systems. Through advocacy & public engagement it highlights how automated task allocation rating systems & opaque decision-making affect workers’ access to employment income stability & due process. The article documents these concerns while the  'Declaración de Asunción sobre el trabajo mediante plataformas en Latinoamérica'(https://www.tedic.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/04/Declaracion-Asuncion-Fairwork.pdf) calls for transparency fairness & accountability in algorithmic systems used by digital labour platforms establishing a clear link between platform work and AI-driven management practices.
cse2_name: EmpleaPY: RESEARCH ON THE AUTOMATION OF PROCESSES FOR EMPLOYMENT POLICIES IN PARAGUAY
cse2_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse2_contributions: Labour Protections
cse2_justif: This report analyses the Paraguayan government’s public employment platform EmpleaPy documenting risks related to automated decision-making in employment services. It highlights concerns about data protection gaps lack of consent mechanisms absence of impact assessments and potential discriminatory effects in automated profiling and matching (p.12-20). The study evaluates regulatory weaknesses and recommends safeguards to protect job seekers’ rights when ADM systems are introduced.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Trust and Safety
cse1_name: Advocacy by a CS coalition for a comprehensive data protection law in Paraguay
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_justif: A CSO coalition integrated by TEDIC APADIC Internet Society Paraguay chapter & AbenteSteward Legal Practice (https://www.datospersonales.org.py/#quienes-somos) has been advocating for the adoption of a comprehensive data protection law in Paraguay. The initiative includes legislative engagement technical input public awareness actions & monitoring of the congressional process to promote stronger privacy safeguards & responsible data processing. It directly contributes to enhancing national data protection standards & accountability in digital governance. To see the evidence's date:https://drive.google.com/file/d/1YjgC6bMfb6KOE77Oc7P_id3hOLwt822S/view?usp=share_link
cse2_name: TEDIC — Bootcamp Asunción 2025 on Digital Rights
cse2_type: Capacity building and collaboration: training courses workshops networks.
cse2_contributions: Data Protection and Privacy;  Safety and Security;  AI-Facilitated Misinformation and Violence
cse2_justif: TEDIC’s Bootcamp Asunción 2025 provided training on digital rights and technology from a human rights perspective. The programme covered topics such as the functioning of the internet privacy and data protection freedom of expression and content moderation basic principles of artificial intelligence online gender-based violence and digital security for activists. It strengthened participants’ capacity to understand AI-related risks algorithmic harms and the impact of automated systems on rights contributing to broader national capacity building on digital safety and responsible technology use in Paraguay.

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Ethics and Sustainability
cse1_name: Comments on the Draft Regulations of Law 31814 a law that promotes the use of AI for the country's economic and social development - Peru
cse1_type: Knowledge generation: research technical reports.
cse1_contributions: Fairness and Non Discrimination;  Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse1_justif: The letter sent by NGO Hiperderecho contains comments on the proposed implementation of the AI Law makes several observations regarding the potential risks of discrimination (p. 9) and includes proposals to better define concepts such as 'vulnerable populations' (p. 12). With respect to transparency it proposes improvements to make AI-based systems explainable including strengthening mechanisms such as public registries of algorithms (p. 6 and 7). Finally it proposes the creation of standards to ensure human oversight when public entities adopt AI-based systems (p. 6).
cse2_name: AI in Peru: Regulations ready data pending
cse2_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse2_contributions: Transparency and Explainability;  Human Oversight and Determination
cse2_justif: The policy report conducted by NGO Hiperderecho review what the AI ??Law Regulations say about data' and it specifically assesses Openness and transparency (p. 6) alongside data quality and human oversight (pp. 2 and 6).

indicator: Civil Society Engagement in Inclusion and Diversity
cse1_name: Comments on the Draft Regulations of Law 31814 a law that promotes the use of AI for the country's economic and social development - Peru
cse1_type: Participation in public policy formulation implementation M&E: as part of advisory councils partnerships with state independent observers participating in parliamentary hearings.
cse1_contributions: Children’s Rights;  Rights of Persons with Disabilities
cse1_justif: Letter sent by the CSOs Access Now and Hiperderecho (p. 1) offers comments on the Draft Regulations of Law 31814. Among these is a proposed expansion of the category of AI systems to be classified as posing an unacceptable risk (p. 10). Specifically they argue that AI systems which exploit the vulnerabilities of specific groups - including vulnerabilities related to age or physical or mental disability - should fall within the unacceptable risk category (p. 11). This proposal would substantially strengthen protections for both children and persons with disabilities as AI systems designed to exploit their specific vulnerabilities would automatically be classified as presenting an unacceptable risk and would therefore be prohibited.
